Each element in ``steps`` is either:\n    59\t    - A single Step (executed sequentially)\n    60\t    - A list[Step] (all steps in the list executed in parallel)\n    61\t\n    62\t    For each step:\n    63\t    1. Call on_step_start(step)\n    64\t    2. Call run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\n    65\t    3. If step has gate criteria: run gate_passed()\n    66\t       - BLOCKING mode: evaluate synchronously, halt on failure\n    67\t       - TRAILING mode (grace_period > 0): submit to TrailingGateRunner,\n    68\t         continue execution; sync point at end collects results\n    69\t    4. If gate fails and attempts < retry_limit: retry (go to 2)\n    70\t    5. If gate fails and attempts exhausted: HALT\n    71\t    6. Call on_step_complete(step, result)\n    72\t    7. Call on_state_update(updated_state_dict)\n    73\t\n    74\t    For parallel step groups:\n    75\t    - All steps in the group run concurrently via _run_parallel_steps()\n    76\t    - If any step in the group fails, remaining steps are cancelled\n    77\t    - All results from the group must be PASS before proceeding\n...\n    86\t\n    87\t    for entry in steps:\n    88\t        if cancel_check():\n    89\t            break\n    90\t\n    91\t        if isinstance(entry, list):\n    92\t            # Enforces monotonicity: consumed can only increase.\n   471\t    \"\"\"\n   472\t\n   473\t    initial_budget: int\n   474\t    consumed: int = 0\n   475\t    reimbursed: int = 0\n   476\t    reimbursement_rate: float = 0.5\n   477\t    minimum_allocation: int = 5\n   478\t    minimum_remediation_budget: int = 3\n   479\t\n   480\t    def available(self) -> int:\n   481\t        \"\"\"Return available turns: initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed.\"\"\"\n   482\t        return self.initial_budget - self.consumed + self.reimbursed\n   483\t\n   484\t    def debit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n   485\t        \"\"\"Consume turns from the budget. Enforces monotonicity.\"\"\"\n   486\t        if turns < 0:\n   487\t            raise ValueError(\"debit amount must be non-negative\")\n   488\t        self.consumed += turns\n   489\t\n   490\t    def credit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n   491\t        \"\"\"Reimburse turns to the budget.\"\"\"\n   492\t        if turns < 0:\n   493\t            raise ValueError(\"credit amount must be non-negative\")\n   494\t        self.reimbursed += turns\n   495\t\n   496\t    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n   497\t        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for a subprocess launch.\"\"\"\n   498\t        return self.available() >= self.minimum_allocation\n   499\t\n   500\t    def can_remediate(self) -> bool:\n   501\t        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for remediation.\"\"\"\n   502\t        return self.available() >= self.minimum_remediation_budget\n   503\t\n   504\t\n   505\tdef build_resume_output(\n   506\t    config: SprintConfig,\n   507\t    halt_task_id: str,\n   508\t    remaining_tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n   509\t    diagnostic_path: str | None = None,\n   510\t    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n   511\t) -> str:\n   512\t    \"\"\"Build actionable HALT output with resume command and context.\n   513\t\n   514\t    Produces an actionable resume block containing:\n   515\t    - Budget check: can we launch?\\\\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\\\\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\\\\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\\\\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\\\\n                results.append(\\\\n                    TaskResult(\\\\n                        task=t,\\\\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\\\\n                        started_at=started_at,\\\\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\\\\n                    )\\\\n                )\\\\n            break\\\\n\\\\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\\\\n        if ledger is not None:\\\\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\\\\n\\\\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\\\\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\\\\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\\\\n                task, config, phase\\\\n            )\\\\n        else:\\\\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\\\\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\\\\n                task, config, phase\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\\\\n\\\\n        # Determine task status from exit code\\\\n        if exit_code == 0:\\\\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\\\\n        elif exit_code == 124:\\\\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\\\\n        else:\\\\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\\\\n\\\\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\\\\n        if ledger is not None:\\\\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\\\\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\\\\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\\\\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\\\\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\\\\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\\\\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\\\\n\\\\n        results.append(\\\\n            TaskResult(\\\\n                task=task,\\\\n                status=status,\\\\n                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\\\\n                exit_code=exit_code,\\\\n                started_at=started_at,\\\\n                finished_at=finished_at,\\\\n                output_bytes=output_bytes,\\\\n            )\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    return results, remaining\\\"}]\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_015SJoXWvJHbokN9UYMmb2Sk","session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"df658302-8aa4-4453-8635-71615b4dc82f"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_wKQDDig3Jt2nrcgcxuzegQgF","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"result\":\"[{\\\"name_path\\\": \\\"execute_pipeline\\\", \\\"kind\\\": \\\"Function\\\", \\\"relative_path\\\": \\\"src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py\\\", \\\"body_location\\\": {\\\"start_line\\\": 45, \\\"end_line\\\": 132}, \\\"body\\\": \\\"def execute_pipeline(\\\\n    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\\\\n    config: PipelineConfig,\\\\n    run_step: StepRunner,\\\\n    on_step_start: Callable[[Step], None] = lambda s: None,\\\\n    on_step_complete: Callable[[Step, StepResult], None] = lambda s, r: None,\\\\n    on_state_update: Callable[[dict], None] = lambda state: None,\\\\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool] = lambda: False,\\\\n    trailing_runner: TrailingGateRunner | None = None,\\\\n) -> list[StepResult]:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Generic pipeline executor.\\\\n\\\\n    Processes steps in order. Each element in ``steps`` is either:\\\\n    - A single Step (executed sequentially)\\\\n    - A list[Step] (all steps in the list executed in parallel)\\\\n\\\\n    For each step:\\\\n    1. Call on_step_start(step)\\\\n    2. Call run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\\\\n    3. If step has gate criteria: run gate_passed()\\\\n       - BLOCKING mode: evaluate synchronously, halt on failure\\\\n       - TRAILING mode (grace_period > 0): submit to TrailingGateRunner,\\\\n         continue execution; sync point at end collects results\\\\n    4. If gate fails and attempts < retry_limit: retry (go to 2)\\\\n    5. If gate fails and attempts exhausted: HALT\\\\n    6. Call on_step_complete(step, result)\\\\n    7. Call on_state_update(updated_state_dict)\\\\n\\\\n    For parallel step groups:\\\\n    - All steps in the group run concurrently via _run_parallel_steps()\\\\n    - If any step in the group fails, remaining steps are cancelled\\\\n    - All results from the group must be PASS before proceeding\\\\n\\\\n    Returns list of all StepResults (one per step, flattened).\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    all_results: list[StepResult] = []\\\\n    # Create trailing runner if grace_period > 0 and none provided\\\\n    _trailing = trailing_runner\\\\n    if _trailing is None and config.grace_period > 0:\\\\n        _trailing = TrailingGateRunner()\\\\n\\\\n    for entry in steps:\\\\n        if cancel_check():\\\\n            break\\\\n\\\\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\\\\n            # Parallel step group\\\\n            for s in entry:\\\\n                on_step_start(s)\\\\n\\\\n            group_results = _run_parallel_steps(entry, config, run_step, cancel_check)\\\\n            all_results.extend(group_results)\\\\n\\\\n            for r in group_results:\\\\n                on_step_complete(r.step, r)\\\\n\\\\n            on_state_update(_build_state(all_results))\\\\n\\\\n            # If any step in the group failed, halt\\\\n            if any(r.status != StepStatus.PASS for r in group_results):\\\\n                break\\\\n        else:\\\\n            # Sequential step -- branch on gate_mode\\\\n            result = _execute_single_step(\\\\n                entry, config, run_step, cancel_check,\\\\n                on_step_start, on_step_complete,\\\\n                trailing_runner=_trailing,\\\\n            )\\\\n            all_results.append(result)\\\\n            on_state_update(_build_state(all_results))\\\\n\\\\n            if result.status != StepStatus.PASS:\\\\n                break\\\\n\\\\n    # Sync point: collect all trailing gate results at end of pipeline\\\\n    if _trailing is not None:\\\\n        trailing_results = _trailing.wait_for_pending(\\\\n            timeout=max(30.0, float(config.grace_period))\\\\n        )\\\\n        for tr in trailing_results:\\\\n            if not tr.passed:\\\\n                _log.warning(\\\\n                    \\\\\\\"Trailing gate failed for step '%s': %s\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    tr.step_id,\\\\n                    tr.failure_reason,\\\\n                )\\\\n\\\\n    return all_results\\\"}]\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_015SJoXWvJHbokN9UYMmb2Sk","session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"a248efe0-55a3-46bf-a1c1-ddedbf09399d"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_KNeJmmFlczNpW7oHchIZeugv","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"result\":\"[{\\\"name_path\\\": \\\"_execute_single_step\\\", \\\"kind\\\": \\\"Function\\\", \\\"relative_path\\\": \\\"src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py\\\", \\\"body_location\\\": {\\\"start_line\\\": 135, \\\"end_line\\\": 249}, \\\"body\\\": \\\"def _execute_single_step(\\\\n    step: Step,\\\\n    config: PipelineConfig,\\\\n    run_step: StepRunner,\\\\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\\\\n    on_step_start: Callable[[Step], None] = lambda s: None,\\\\n    on_step_complete: Callable[[Step, StepResult], None] = lambda s, r: None,\\\\n    trailing_runner: TrailingGateRunner | None = None,\\\\n) -> StepResult:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Execute a single step with retry logic and gate checking.\\\\n\\\\n    Gate mode branching:\\\\n    - BLOCKING (default): gate evaluates synchronously before proceeding\\\\n    - TRAILING (grace_period > 0): gate submitted to TrailingGateRunner,\\\\n      step returns PASS immediately and execution continues\\\\n    - grace_period == 0 forces BLOCKING regardless of gate_mode\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    on_step_start(step)\\\\n    max_attempts = step.retry_limit + 1  # retry_limit=1 means 2 attempts total\\\\n\\\\n    # Determine effective gate mode\\\\n    effective_mode = step.gate_mode\\\\n    if config.grace_period == 0:\\\\n        effective_mode = GateMode.BLOCKING\\\\n\\\\n    for attempt in range(1, max_attempts + 1):\\\\n        if cancel_check():\\\\n            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\\\\n            result = StepResult(\\\\n                step=step,\\\\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\\\\n                attempt=attempt,\\\\n                gate_failure_reason=\\\\\\\"Cancelled by external signal\\\\\\\",\\\\n                started_at=now,\\\\n                finished_at=now,\\\\n            )\\\\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\\\\n            return result\\\\n\\\\n        result = run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\\\\n        result = StepResult(\\\\n            step=result.step,\\\\n            status=result.status,\\\\n            attempt=attempt,\\\\n            gate_failure_reason=result.gate_failure_reason,\\\\n            started_at=result.started_at,\\\\n            finished_at=result.finished_at,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n        # If step has no gate, trust the StepRunner's status\\\\n        if step.gate is None:\\\\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\\\\n            return result\\\\n\\\\n        # If runner already reported failure/timeout, skip gate check\\\\n        if result.status in (StepStatus.TIMEOUT, StepStatus.CANCELLED):\\\\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\\\\n            return result\\\\n\\\\n        # TRAILING mode: submit to runner, return PASS immediately\\\\n        if effective_mode == GateMode.TRAILING and trailing_runner is not None:\\\\n            trailing_runner.submit(step)\\\\n            result = StepResult(\\\\n                step=step,\\\\n                status=StepStatus.PASS,\\\\n                attempt=attempt,\\\\n                gate_failure_reason=None,\\\\n                started_at=result.started_at,\\\\n                finished_at=result.finished_at,\\\\n            )\\\\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\\\\n            return result\\\\n\\\\n        # BLOCKING mode: run gate check synchronously\\\\n        passed, reason = gate_passed(step.output_file, step.gate)\\\\n        if passed:\\\\n            result = StepResult(\\\\n                step=step,\\\\n                status=StepStatus.PASS,\\\\n                attempt=attempt,\\\\n                gate_failure_reason=None,\\\\n                started_at=result.started_at,\\\\n                finished_at=result.finished_at,\\\\n            )\\\\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\\\\n            return result\\\\n\\\\n        # Gate failed\\\\n        _log.info(\\\\\\\"Gate failed for step '%s' (attempt %d/%d): %s\\\\\\\", step.id, attempt, max_attempts, reason)\\\\n\\\\n        if attempt < max_attempts:\\\\n            continue  # retry\\\\n\\\\n        # Exhausted retries\\\\n        result = StepResult(\\\\n            step=step,\\\\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\\\\n            attempt=attempt,\\\\n            gate_failure_reason=reason,\\\\n            started_at=result.started_at,\\\\n            finished_at=result.finished_at,\\\\n        )\\\\n        on_step_complete(step, result)\\\\n        return result\\\\n\\\\n    # Should not reach here, but safety fallback\\\\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\\\\n    return StepResult(\\\\n        step=step,\\\\n        status=StepStatus.FAIL,\\\\n        attempt=max_attempts,\\\\n        gate_failure_reason=\\\\\\\"Unexpected: exhausted retry loop\\\\\\\",\\\\n        started_at=now,\\\\n        finished_at=now,\\\\n    )\\\"}]\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_015SJoXWvJHbokN9UYMmb2Sk","session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"8fac1db1-6f89-4ed3-b91e-1b71788ffa72"}

Gate outcome summary\\\\n    sections.append(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n### Gate Outcomes\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n    for result in prior_results:\\\\n        sections.append(\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"- {result.task.task_id}: {result.gate_outcome.value}\\\\\\\"\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    # Git diff context\\\\n    if start_commit:\\\\n        diff_section = get_git_diff_context(start_commit)\\\\n        if diff_section:\\\\n            sections.append(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n{diff_section}\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(sections) + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\"}]\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_015SJoXWvJHbokN9UYMmb2Sk","session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"23fd2a5f-3637-46be-83b2-baaa63fc0687"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_vXFcgmnChv29WQsS7alCGVDn","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"result\":\"[{\\\"name_path\\\": \\\"attempt_remediation\\\", \\\"kind\\\": \\\"Function\\\", \\\"relative_path\\\": \\\"src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py\\\", \\\"body_location\\\": {\\\"start_line\\\": 357, \\\"end_line\\\": 450}, \\\"body\\\": \\\"def attempt_remediation(\\\\n    remediation_step: Step,\\\\n    turns_per_attempt: int,\\\\n    can_remediate: Callable[[], bool],\\\\n    debit: Callable[[int], None],\\\\n    run_step: Callable[[Step], StepResult],\\\\n    check_gate: Callable[[StepResult], TrailingGateResult],\\\\n) -> RemediationRetryResult:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Execute remediation with retry-once semantics and budget integration.\\\\n\\\\n    State machine:\\\\n    - Pre-check: can_remediate() → False → BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\\\\n    - ATTEMPT_1: debit → run → gate → pass → PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT\\\\n    - ATTEMPT_1 fail: can_remediate() check → False → PERSISTENT_FAILURE (1 attempt debited)\\\\n    - ATTEMPT_2: debit → run → gate → pass → PASS_SECOND_ATTEMPT\\\\n    - ATTEMPT_2 fail: PERSISTENT_FAILURE (both attempts debited)\\\\n\\\\n    Both attempts' turns are consumed on persistent failure (no reimbursement).\\\\n\\\\n    Args:\\\\n        remediation_step: The Step to execute for remediation.\\\\n        turns_per_attempt: Turns to debit per attempt.\\\\n        can_remediate: Callable returning True if budget allows remediation.\\\\n        debit: Callable to consume turns from budget.\\\\n        run_step: Callable to execute the remediation step.\\\\n        check_gate: Callable to evaluate gate on step result.\\\\n\\\\n    Returns:\\\\n        RemediationRetryResult with status and accounting.\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    total_turns = 0\\\\n\\\\n    # Pre-check: enough budget for first attempt?\\\\n    if not can_remediate():\\\\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\\\\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.BUDGET_EXHAUSTED,\\\\n            attempts_made=0,\\\\n            turns_consumed=0,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    # --- Attempt 1 ---\\\\n    debit(turns_per_attempt)\\\\n    total_turns += turns_per_attempt\\\\n    result_1 = run_step(remediation_step)\\\\n    gate_1 = check_gate(result_1)\\\\n\\\\n    if gate_1.passed:\\\\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\\\\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT,\\\\n            attempts_made=1,\\\\n            turns_consumed=total_turns,\\\\n            final_gate_result=gate_1,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    _log.info(\\\\n        \\\\\\\"Remediation attempt 1 for '%s' failed: %s\\\\\\\",\\\\n        remediation_step.id, gate_1.failure_reason,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    # Pre-check before attempt 2\\\\n    if not can_remediate():\\\\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\\\\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.PERSISTENT_FAILURE,\\\\n            attempts_made=1,\\\\n            turns_consumed=total_turns,\\\\n            final_gate_result=gate_1,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    # --- Attempt 2 ---\\\\n    debit(turns_per_attempt)\\\\n    total_turns += turns_per_attempt\\\\n    result_2 = run_step(remediation_step)\\\\n    gate_2 = check_gate(result_2)\\\\n\\\\n    if gate_2.passed:\\\\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\\\\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.PASS_SECOND_ATTEMPT,\\\\n            attempts_made=2,\\\\n            turns_consumed=total_turns,\\\\n            final_gate_result=gate_2,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    _log.info(\\\\n        \\\\\\\"Remediation attempt 2 for '%s' failed (persistent): %s\\\\\\\",\\\\n        remediation_step.id, gate_2.failure_reason,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    # Persistent failure: both attempts' turns lost\\\\n    return RemediationRetryResult(\\\\n        status=RemediationRetryStatus.PERSISTENT_FAILURE,\\\\n        attempts_made=2,\\\\n        turns_consumed=total_turns,\\\\n        final_gate_result=gate_2,\\\\n    )\\\"}]\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_015SJoXWvJHbokN9UYMmb2Sk","session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"46badaa7-d820-4339-b5a9-8a8d0cae1764"}

87→\n    88→class ClaudeProcess(_PipelineClaudeProcess):\n    89→    \"\"\"Sprint-specific claude process extending the pipeline base.\n    90→\n    91→    Defines only __init__ (with hook wiring) and build_prompt().\n    92→    All subprocess lifecycle (start, wait, terminate) is inherited\n    93→    from the pipeline base class; sprint debug logging is injected\n    94→    via lifecycle hook factories.\n    95→    \"\"\"\n    96→\n    97→    def __init__(self, config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase):\n    98→        self.config = config\n    99→        self.phase = phase\n   100→     timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n   109→            output_format=\"stream-json\",\n   110→            on_spawn=_make_spawn_hook(phase, config),\n   111→ \"\"\"Build the /sc:task-unified prompt for this phase.\"\"\"\n   117→        pn = self.phase.number\n   118→        result_file = self.config.result_file(self.phase)\n   119→        phase_file = self.phase.file\n   120→\n   121→        return (\n   122→            f\"/sc:task-unified Execute all tasks in @{phase_file} \"\n   123→            f\"--compliance strict --strategy systematic\\n\"\n   124→            f\"\\n\"\n   125→            f\"## Execution Rules\\n\"\n   126→            f\"- Execute tasks in order (T{pn:02d}XX.01, T{pn:02d}XX.02, etc.)\\n\"\n   127→            f\"- For STRICT tier tasks: use Sequential MCP for analysis, \"\n   128→            f\"run quality verification\\n\"\n   129→            f\"- For STANDARD tier tasks: run direct test execution per \"\n   130→            f\"acceptance criteria\\n\"\n   131→            f\"- For LIGHT tier tasks: quick sanity check only\\n\"\n   132→            f\"- For EXEMPT tier tasks: skip formal verification\\n\"\n   133→            f\"- If a STRICT-tier task fails, STOP and report -- \"\n   134→            f\"do not continue to next task\\n\"\n   135→            f\"- For all other tier failures, log the failure and continue\\n\"\n   136→            f\"\\n\"\n   137→            f\"## Completion Protocol\\n\"\n   138→            f\"When ALL tasks in this phase are complete \"\n   139→            f\"(or halted on STRICT failure):\\n\"\n   140→            f\"1. Write a phase completion report to {result_file} containing:\\n\"\n   141→            f\"   - YAML frontmatter with: phase, status (PASS|FAIL|PARTIAL), \"\n   142→            f\"tasks_total, tasks_passed, tasks_failed\\n\"\n   143→            f\"   - Per-task status table: Task ID, Title, Tier, Status \"\n   144→            f\"(pass/fail/skip), Evidence\\n\"\n   145→            f\"   - Files modified (list all paths)\\n\"\n   146→            f\"   - Blockers for next phase (if any)\\n\"\n   147→            f\"   - The literal string EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE \"\n   148→            f\"or EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\n\"\n   149→            f\"2. If any task produced file changes, list them under \"\n   150→            f\"## Files Modified\\n\"\n   151→            f\"\\n\"\n   152→            f\"## Important\\n\"\n   153→            f\"- This is Phase {pn} of a multi-phase sprint\\n\"\n   154→            f\"- Previous phases have already been executed in separate sessions\\n\"\n   155→            f\"- Do not re-execute work from prior phases\\n\"\n   156→            f\"- Focus only on the tasks defined in the phase file\"\n   157→        )\n   158→\n   159→\n   160→class SignalHandler:\n   161→    \"\"\"Register signal handlers for graceful sprint shutdown.\n   162→\n   163→    On SIGINT/SIGTERM:\n   164→    1. Set the shutdown flag (checked by the executor loop)\n   165→    2. The executor terminates the current claude process\n   166→    3. Writes partial execution log\n   167→    4. Exits with appropriate code\n   168→    \"\"\"\n   169→\n   170→    def __init__(self):\n   171→        self.shutdown_requested = False\n   172→        self._original_sigint = None\n   173→        self._original_sigterm = None\n   174→\n   175→    def install(self):\n   176→        \"\"\"Install signal handlers.\"\"\"\n   177→        self._original_sigint = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGINT)\n   178→        self._original_sigterm = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)\n   179→        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._handle)\n   180→        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._handle)\n   181→\n   182→    def uninstall(self):\n   183→        \"\"\"Restore original signal handlers.\"\"\"\n   184→        if self._original_sigint is not None:\n   185→            signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._original_sigint)\n   186→        if self._original_sigterm is not None:\n   187→            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._original_sigterm)\n   188→\n   189→    def _handle(self, signum, frame):\n   190→        self.shutdown_requested = True\n   191→\n   192→\n   193→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   194→# Context Injection\n   195→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   196→\n   197→\n   198→def build_task_context(\n   199→    prior_results: list[TaskResult],\n   200→    *,\n   201→    start_commit: str = \"\",\n   202→    compress_threshold: int = 3,\n   203→) -> str:\n   204→    \"\"\"Build deterministic context from prior task results for injection into prompts.\n   205→\n   206→    Produces structured markdown containing:\n   207→    - Prior task results (status, gate outcome, key details)\n   208→    - Gate outcomes (pass/fail/deferred per prior task)\n   209→    - Remediation history (reimbursement amounts)\n   210→    - Git diff summary (if start_commit is provided)\n   211→\n   212→    Context is compressed via progressive summarization when the number of\n   213→    prior results exceeds ``compress_threshold`` (default 3): older tasks\n   214→    are reduced to a one-line summary while recent tasks retain full detail.\n   215→\n   216→    Args:\n   217→        prior_results: Ordered list of TaskResult from preceding tasks.\n   218→        start_commit: Git commit ref for diff context (empty = skip).\n   219→        compress_threshold: Number of recent tasks to keep at full detail.\n   220→\n   221→    Returns:\n   222→        Structured markdown string for inclusion in task prompts.\n   223→    \"\"\"\n   224→    if not prior_results:\n   225→        return \"\"\n   226→\n   227→    sections: list[str] = [\"## Prior Task Context\\n\"]\n   228→\n   229→    # Apply progressive summarization\n   230→    if len(prior_results) > compress_threshold:\n   231→        compressed = compress_context_summary(prior_results, keep_recent=compress_threshold)\n   232→        sections.append(compressed)\n   233→    else:\n   234→        for result in GateResultQueue:\n    45→    \"\"\"Thread-safe queue for collecting gate evaluation results.\n    46→\n    47→    Wraps stdlib ``queue.Queue`` with typed put/drain/pending_count methods.\n    48→    Safe for concurrent access from multiple daemon threads.\n    49→    \"\"\"\n    50→\n    51→    def __init__(self) -> None:\n    52→        self._queue: Queue[TrailingGateResult] = Queue()\n    53→ Thread-safe.\"\"\"\n    58→        self._queue.put(result)\n    59→        with self._lock:\n    60→            self._pending += 1\n    61→\n    62→    def drain(self) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n    63→        \"\"\"Collect all available results without blocking.\n    64→\n    65→        Returns a list of all results currently in the queue.\n    66→        \"\"\"\n    67→        results: list[TrailingGateResult] = []\n    68→        while True:\n    69→            try:\n    70→                result = self._queue.get_nowait()\n    71→                results.append(result)\n    72→            except Empty:\n    73→                break\n    74→        with self._lock:\n    75→            self._pending = max(0, self._pending - len(results))\n    76→        return results\n    77→\n    78→    def pending_count(self) -> int:\n    79→        \"\"\"Return count of unprocessed results.\"\"\"\n    80→        return self._queue.qsize()\n    81→\n    82→\n    83→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    84→# T05.01 -- TrailingGateRunner\n    85→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    86→\n    87→\n    88→class TrailingGateRunner:\n    89→    \"\"\"Daemon-thread gate evaluator for trailing gate mode.\n    90→\n    91→    Spawns daemon threads to evaluate gate criteria on step output files\n    92→    after the step subprocess completes. Results are collected via the\n    93→    internal GateResultQueue.\n    94→\n    95→    Thread safety: submit() and drain() may be called concurrently from\n    96→    any thread. cancel() propagates to all active daemon threads.\n    97→    \"\"\"\n    98→\n    99→    def __init__(self, result_queue: GateResultQueue | None = None) -> None:\n   100→        self._queue = result_queue or GateResultQueue()\n   101→        self._threads: list[threading.Thread] = []\n   102→        self._cancelled = threading.Event()\n   103→        self._pending_count = 0\n   104→        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n   105→\n   106→    @property\n   107→    def result_queue(self) -> GateResultQueue:\n   108→        return self._queue\n   109→\n   110→    def submit(\n   111→        self,\n   112→        step: Step,\n   113→        gate_check: Callable[[Path, GateCriteria], tuple[bool, str | None]] = gate_passed,\n   114→    ) -> None:\n   115→        \"\"\"Spawn a daemon thread to evaluate gate criteria on step output.\n   116→\n   117→        The thread terminates after evaluation completes or cancellation.\n   118→        \"\"\"\n   119→        if step.gate is None:\n   120→                    passed=True,\n   125→                    evaluation_ms=0.0,\n   126→                    failure_reason=None,\n   127→                )\n   128→            )\n   129→ 148→                _log.debug(\n   149→                    \"Trailing gate for '%s': %s (%.1fms)\",\n   150→                    step.id,\n   151→                    \"PASS\" if passed else \"FAIL\",\n   152→                    elapsed_ms,\n   153→                )\n   154→            except Exception:\n   155→                _log.exception(\"Trailing gate evaluation error for step '%s'\", step.id)\n   156→                self._queue.put(\n   157→                    TrailingGateResult(\n   158→                        step_id=step.id,\n   159→                        passed=False,\n   160→                        evaluation_ms=0.0,\n   161→                        failure_reason=\"Gate evaluation raised an exception\",\n   162→                    )\n   163→                )\n   164→            finally:\n   165→                with self._lock:\n   166→                    self._pending_count -= 1\n   167→\n   168→        t = threading.Thread(target=_evaluate, daemon=True)\n   169→        self._threads.append(t)\n   170→        t.start()\n   171→\n   172→    def drain(self) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n   173→        \"\"\"Collect all completed gate results.\"\"\"\n   174→        return self._queue.drain()\n   175→\n   176→    def wait_for_pending(self, timeout: float = 30.0) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n   177→        \"\"\"Block until all pending gate evaluations complete or timeout.\n   178→\n   179→        Returns all accumulated results. Never hangs indefinitely.\n   180→        \"\"\"\n   181→        deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout\n   182→        # Join all active threads with bounded timeout\n   183→        for t in list(self._threads):\n   184→            remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()\n   185→            if remaining <= 0:\n   186→                _log.warning(\"wait_for_pending timed out with %d threads still active\", self._pending_count)\n   187→                break\n   188→            t.join(timeout=remaining)\n   189→\n   190→        # Drain whatever results are available\n   191→ event and terminate gracefully.\n   197→        \"\"\"\n   198→        self._cancelled.set()\n   199→        # Give threads a brief window to notice cancellation\n   200→        for t in entrypoint\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/commands.py`:\n- `sprint_group()`\n- `run()`\n- `attach()`\n- `status()`\n- `logs()`\n- `kill()`\n\n`run()` gathers CLI flags such as:\n- phase range\n- `max_turns`\n- model\n- tmux settings\n- stall watchdog settings\n- `--shadow-gates`\n\nIt passes those into sprint config loading.\n\n#### Config and task description\n\n#### Core executor\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py`:\n- `execute_sprint()`\n- `_determine_phase_status()`\n- `execute_phase_tasks()`\n- `aggregate_task_results()`\n- `check_budget_guard()`\n- `setup_isolation()`\n\n`execute_sprint()` is the main orchestration loop:\n1. verify `claude` exists in PATH\n2. install `SignalHandler`\n3. initialize logger, TUI, monitor\n4. iterate over `config.active_phases`\n5. for each phase:\n   - reset `OutputMonitor`\n   - optionally update tmux tail pane\n   - create `ClaudeProcess(config, phase)`\n   - start subprocess\n   - poll until exit or timeout\n   - update TUI continuously\n   - apply stall watchdog behavior\n   - stop monitor\n   - compute `PhaseStatus` via `_determine_phase_status()`\n   - build `PhaseResult`\n   - log + notify\n   - halt on failure and optionally generate diagnostics\n6. write summary and sentinel exit code\n\nImportant: this path is phase-oriented, not yet obviously routed through the generic pipeline executor.\n\n#### Process layer\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/process.py`:\n- `ClaudeProcess`\n- `SignalHandler`\n- `build_task_context()`\n- `get_git_diff_context()`\n- `compress_context_summary()`\n\n`ClaudeProcess` extends the generic pipeline process class and mainly contributes:\n- sprint-specific constructor wiring\n- prompt construction via `build_prompt()`\n\n`build_prompt()` tells Claude to run:\n- `/sc:task-unified Execute all tasks in @phase-file --compliance strict --strategy systematic`\n\nand instructs it to produce:\n- YAML frontmatter\n- per-task status table\n- files modified\n- blockers\n- `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT`\n\n#### `count_turns_from_output()`\n\n`OutputMonitor` tails the stream-json output file in a background thread and extracts:\n- last task id\n- last tool used\n- files changed\n- growth rate\n- stall timing\n- output byte count\n\n### Key sprint classes and methods\nFrom `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py`:\n- `SprintConfig`\n  - `active_phases()`\n  - Pipeline/gate system\n\n### Main modules\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/models.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py`\n\n### Generic pipeline executor\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py`:\n- `execute_pipeline()`\n- `_execute_single_step()`\n- `_run_parallel_steps()`\n- `_build_state()`\n\n#### `execute_pipeline()`\nThis is a reusable generic step sequencer:\n- accepts `list[Step | list[Step]]`\n- supports sequential steps and parallel step groups\n- delegates actual execution to injected `run_step`\n- invokes callbacks:\n  - `on_step_start`\n  - `on_step_complete`\n  - `on_state_update`\n- creates a `TrailingGateRunner` automatically if `config.grace_period > 0`\n- waits for trailing results at the end via `wait_for_pending()`\n\n#### `_execute_single_step()`\nCore behavior:\n- retry loop based on `retry_limit`\n- if no gate: trust `StepRunner` result\n- if timeout/cancelled: skip gate checking\n- if effective mode is trailing: submit to `TrailingGateRunner`, mark step PASS immediately\n- if blocking: run `gate_passed()` synchronously\n- if blocking gate fails and retries remain: retry\n- else return FAIL\n\n### Gate evaluation\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py`:\n- `gate_passed()`\n- `_check_frontmatter()`\n\n`gate_passed()` enforces increasing strictness by enforcement tier:\n- `EXEMPT`: always pass\n- `LIGHT`: file exists, non-empty\n- `STANDARD`: plus min line count and required frontmatter\n- `STRICT`: plus semantic checks\n\n### Data model\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/models.py`:\n- `Step`\n- `StepResult`\n- `PipelineConfig`\n- `GateCriteria`\n- `GateMode`\n- `SemanticCheck`\n- `StepStatus`\n\nImportant fields:\n- `Step.id`\n- `Step.prompt`\n- `Step.output_file`\n- `Step.gate`\n- `Step.retry_limit`\n- `Step.gate_mode`\n- `PipelineConfig.grace_period`\n\n### How sprint and pipeline relate\nThere are two overlapping architectures:\n\n1. Current concrete sprint runtime:\n- `execute_sprint()` launches per-phase Claude subprocesses directly.\n\n2. Generic pipeline/gate framework:\n- `execute_pipeline()` is intended as a reusable step engine with blocking/trailing gates.\n\nThere is evidence the repo is moving sprint toward consuming pipeline abstractions:\n- `SprintConfig` is described as inheriting shared pipeline fields\n- sprint imports `Step` and `StepResult`\n- sprint defines `SprintGatePolicy` as a concrete `TrailingGatePolicy`\n- tests cover sprint-facing gate features like display state, KPI reports, context injection, shadow mode\n\nBut in the code I inspected, `execute_sprint()` itself is not yet clearly wired through `execute_pipeline()`.\n\n## 3. TurnLedger: budget management\n\n### Main definition\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py`\n\n`TurnLedger` fields:\n- `initial_budget`\n- `consumed`\n- `reimbursed`\n- `reimbursement_rate = 0.5`\n- `minimum_allocation = 5`\n- `minimum_remediation_budget = 3`\n\nMethods:\n- `available()`: `initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed`\n- `debit(turns)`: increments `consumed`\n- `credit(turns)`: increments `reimbursed`\n- `can_launch()`: checks against `minimum_allocation`\n- `can_remediate()`: checks against `minimum_remediation_budget`\n\n### Where it is used\nPrimary sprint budget logic is in:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py`\n\n#### `check_budget_guard()`\nPreflight helper:\n- returns `None` if launch allowed\n- otherwise returns budget exhaustion message\n\n#### `execute_phase_tasks()`\nPer-task orchestration loop with economic accounting:\n- before each task:\n  - if no launch budget, mark remaining tasks skipped and stop\n- pre-debit `minimum_allocation`\n- run subprocess\n- classify task status from exit code:\n  - `0` -> PASS\n  - `124` -> INCOMPLETE\n  - other -> FAIL\n- reconcile preallocation against actual turn count:\n  - if actual > preallocated -> additional debit\n  - if actual < preallocated -> credit difference\n\nThis is the clearest concrete use of debit/credit logic in the sprint code.\n\n### Related budget/remediation semantics\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py`:\n- `attempt_remediation()` integrates with any budget source through injected callables:\n  - `can_remediate`\n  - `debit`\n\nThis makes remediation accounting pipeline-generic while still allowing sprint to use `TurnLedger`.\n\n## 4. Gate result queue and deferred remediation log\n\n### Gate result queue\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py`:\n- `TrailingGateResult`\n- `GateResultQueue`\n\n`GateResultQueue` is a thread-safe wrapper around stdlib `Queue`:\n- `put(result)`\n- `drain()`\n- `pending_count()`\n\nPurpose:\n- collect gate evaluation results from concurrent daemon threads\n- allow non-blocking draining by consumers\n\n### Trailing gate runner\nAlso in `trailing_gate.py`:\n- `TrailingGateRunner`\n\nKey methods:\n- `submit(step, gate_check=gate_passed)`\n- `drain()`\n- `wait_for_pending(timeout=30.0)`\n- `cancel()`\n- `pending_count`\n\nBehavior:\n- if `step.gate is None`, queue immediate pass result\n- otherwise spawn daemon thread\n- evaluate gate on `step.output_file`\n- record elapsed evaluation time in ms\n- push `TrailingGateResult` into queue\n- support cancellation and bounded waiting\n\n### Deferred remediation log\nIn the same file:\n- `DeferredRemediationLog`\n- `RemediationEntry`\n- `RemediationStatus`\n\nKey methods:\n- `append(gate_result)`\n- `pending_remediations()`\n- `mark_remediated(step_id)`\n- `serialize()`\n- `deserialize()`\n- `_write_to_disk()`\n- `load_from_disk(path)`\n- `entry_count`\n\nPurpose:\n- persist failed gate results that need remediation\n- support resume/recovery via JSON on disk\n- separate unresolved vs remediated items\n\n### Remediation helpers\nStill in `trailing_gate.py`:\n- `build_remediation_prompt()`\n- `attempt_remediation()`\n- `resolve_gate_mode()`\n\n#### `build_remediation_prompt()`\nBuilds a focused prompt including:\n- gate failure reason\n- original acceptance criteria\n- optional file paths\n- explicit instruction to fix only the gate issue\n\n#### `attempt_remediation()`\nTwo-attempt state machine:\n- check remediation budget\n- debit each attempt\n- run remediation step\n- re-check gate\n- return status:\n  - `PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT`\n  - `PASS_SECOND_ATTEMPT`\n  - `PERSISTENT_FAILURE`\n  - `BUDGET_EXHAUSTED`\n\n#### `resolve_gate_mode()`\nScope-based rules:\n- release gates: always blocking\n- milestone gates: configurable\n- task gates: trailing if `grace_period > 0`, else blocking\n\n## 5. Existing test structure\n\n## Sprint tests\nDirectory:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/`\n\nMost relevant files:\n- `test_executor.py`\n- `test_models.py`\n- reconciliation\n- isolation setup\n- aggregation to `AggregatedPhaseReport`\n- YAML/markdown report output expectations\n- backward compatibility defaults\n\n#### `test_models.py`\nCovers:\n- enums extraction patterns\n- background polling\n- incremental reads\n- `detect_error_max_turns()`\n- `count_turns_from_output()`\n\n#### `test_process.py`\nCovers:\n- `ClaudeProcess.build_command()`\n- `build_env()`\n- `build_prompt()`\n- timeout math\n- OS fallback process behavior\n- signal handler install/uninstall\n- task context helpers\n\n#### `test_context_injection.py`\nVery detailed behavioral tests for:\n- single-task context\n- many-task context\n- progressive summarization\n- gate outcomes\n- remediation history\n\n#### `test_gate_display_state.py`\nCovers TUI-facing gate lifecycle modeling:\n- allowed transitions\n- enum values\n- display properties\n\n#### `test_shadow_mode.py`\nCovers:\n- config flag only\n- passive metrics collection via `ShadowGateMetrics`\n- `DeferredRemediationLog`\n\n## Pipeline tests\nDirectory:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/pipeline/`\n\nMost relevant files:\n- `test_executor.py`\n- `test_trailing_gate.py`\n- `test_thread_safety.py`\n- `test_full_flow.py`\n- `test_gate_performance.py`\n- `conftest.py`\n\n### What pipeline tests cover\n#### `test_executor.py`\nCovers:\n- sequential step execution\n- no-gate behavior\n- retry on gate failure\n- pass on retry\n- callback order\n- halt-on-failure semantics\n- cancellation behavior\n- retry exhaustion\n\n#### `test_trailing_gate.py`\nCovers:\n- remediation prompt construction\n- remediation retry logic\n\n#### `test_thread_safety.py`\nHeavy concurrency tests for:\n- queue correctness under concurrent put/drain\n- runner correctness under concurrent submit\n- step_id isolation\n- cancellation timing\n- repeated stability\n\n#### `test_full_flow.py`\nThis is the best architectural intent test.\nIt exercises scenario-level flows:\n1. gate pass -> continue\n2. gate fail -> remediation pass -> continue\n3. gate fail -> remediation fails twice -> HALT + diagnostics + resume output\n4. low budget -> remediation blocked -> HALT\n\nIt composes:\n- `TurnLedger`\n- `build_remediation_prompt()`\n- `attempt_remediation()`\n- `review_conflicts()`\n- `run_diagnostic_chain()`\n- `build_resume_output()`\n\n#### `test_gate_performance.py`\nNon-functional tests:\n- gate evaluation under 100KB input\n- latency population\n- deterministic behavior\n- no-gate fast path\n\n## Integration map\n\n### Sprint runtime path\n- CLI `run()` in `commands.py`\n  -> `load_sprint_config()` in `config.py`\n  -> `execute_sprint()` in `executor.py`\n  -> `ClaudeProcess` in `process.py`\n  -> subprocess output watched by gate path\n- `Step` + `GateCriteria` in `pipeline/models.py`\n  -> `execute_pipeline()`\n  -> `_execute_single_step()`\n  -> either `gate_passed()` synchronously\n     or `TrailingGateRunner.submit()` asynchronously\n  -> trailing results collected via `GateResultQueue`\n  -> failures optionally captured in `DeferredRemediationLog`\n  -> remediation via `build_remediation_prompt()` + `TrailingGatePolicy`\n  - `TrailingGateResult`\n- `SprintGatePolicy` implements the pipeline-owned protocol\n\n### Observability path\n- gate results + remediation log\n  -> Factory helpers instead of fixtures for domain objects\nCommon helper style:\n- `_make_config(...)`\n- `_make_step(...)`\n- `_make_gate(...)`\n- `_make_task_result(...)`\n- `_result(...)`\n\nThese keep tests short and local.\n\n### 2. `tmp_path` for nearly all file-based behavior\nTests heavily use pytest `tmp_path` to:\n- create output files\n- create result files\n- simulate sprint releases\n- validate persistence and reload\n\n### 3. In-memory runner lambdas/callables for orchestration\nPipeline tests usually avoid real subprocesses and instead inject callables:\n- `_make_runner(...)`\n- lambdas returning `StepResult`\n- custom retry runners with mutable counters\n\nThis is the dominant pipeline mocking pattern.\n\n### 4. `unittest.mock.patch` for subprocess and OS behavior\nSprint tests patch:\n- `subprocess.Popen`\n- `shutil.which`\n- OS capability checks like `hasattr(os, \"setpgrp\")`\n- process termination behavior\n\nExample pattern appears in:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_process.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_executor.py`\n\n### 5. Fake process objects instead of real subprocesses\nPattern:\n- tiny fake classes or `MagicMock` with:\n  - `poll()`\n  - `wait()`\n  - `returncode`\n  - `pid`\n\nUsed to drive:\n- pass\n- halt\n- timeout\n- interruption paths\n\n### 6. Real files, mocked orchestration boundaries\nEspecially in pipeline tests:\n- real file contents are written\n- real gate logic is exercised\n- orchestration boundary is mocked\n\nThis gives better confidence than pure unit mocks.\n\n### 7. Concurrency tests use real threads, not mocked threads\nThread-safety tests are real:\n- `threading.Thread`\n- `Barrier`\n- `Event`\n- small sleeps\n- repeated production/drain loops\n\nThis is important because the queue/runner semantics depend on real scheduling.\n\n## Existing test idioms worth noting\n\n- Tests are organized by behavior class, not by free-floating functions.\n- Many tests have contract-style names:\n  - “must produce”\n  - “does not affect”\n  - “halts”\n  - “reclassifies”\n- Regression tests are clearly called out in docstrings/comments.\n- There is good coverage of edge cases:\n  - case-insensitive result signals\n  - missing files\n  - malformed NDJSON\n  - timeout precedence\n  - exact threshold budgets\n  - cross-thread contamination\n\n## Important findings / caveats\n\n### 1. The gate system is well-developed as a generic framework\nThe pipeline side is coherent:\n- `Step`\n- `GateCriteria`\n- `execute_pipeline()`\n- trailing evaluation\n- remediation prompt/retry\n- persistence\n\n### 2. The sprint runner is only partially converged with that framework\nSprint has:\n- pipeline-shaped config/model overlap\n- a `SprintGatePolicy`\n- shadow gate metrics\n- KPI reporting based on gate results\n- tests for gate-related UI and observability\n\nBut the concrete `execute_sprint()` path I inspected still runs:\n- per-phase subprocesses\n- phase result parsing\n- direct monitor/TUI entrypoint\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/commands.py`:\n- `sprint_group()`\n- `run()`\n- `attach()`\n- `status()`\n- `logs()`\n- `kill()`\n\n`run()` gathers install `SignalHandler`\n3. initialize logger, TUI, monitor\n4. iterate over `config.active_phases`\n5. for each phase:\n   - reset `OutputMonitor`\n   - optionally update tmux tail pane\n   - create `ClaudeProcess(config, phase)`\n   - start subprocess\n   - poll until exit or timeout\n   - update TUI continuously\n   - apply stall watchdog behavior\n   - stop monitor\n   - compute `PhaseStatus` via `_determine_phase_status()`\n   - build `PhaseResult`\n   - log + notify\n   - halt on failure and optionally generate diagnostics\n6. write summary and sentinel exit code\n\nImportant: this path is phase-oriented, not yet obviously routed through the generic pipeline executor.\n\n#### Process layer\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/process.py`:\n- `ClaudeProcess`\n- `SignalHandler`\n- `build_task_context()`\n- `get_git_diff_context()`\n- `compress_context_summary()`\n\n`ClaudeProcess` extends the generic pipeline process class and mainly contributes:\n- sprint-specific constructor wiring\n- prompt construction via `build_prompt()`\n\n`build_prompt()` tells Claude to run:\n- `/sc:task-unified Execute all tasks in @phase-file --compliance strict --strategy systematic`\n\nand instructs it to produce:\n- YAML frontmatter\n- per-task status table\n- files modified\n- blockers\n- `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT`\n\n#### Output monitoring\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/monitor.py`:\n- `OutputMonitor`\n- `detect_error_max_turns()`\n- `count_turns_from_output()`\n\n`OutputMonitor` tails the stream-json output file in a background thread and extracts:\n- last task id\n- last tool used\n- files changed\n- growth rate\n- stall timing\n- output byte count\n\n### Key sprint classes and methods\nFrom `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py`:\n- `SprintConfig`\n  - `active_phases()`\n  - `duration_seconds`\n  - `duration_display`\n- `SprintResult`\n  - `phases_passed`\n  - `phases_failed`\n  - `resume_command`\n- `MonitorState`\n  - `stall_status`\n  - `output_size_display`\n- `TaskEntry`\n- `TaskResult`\n  - `duration_seconds`\n  - `to_context_summary()`\n- `TurnLedger`\n  - `available()`\n  - `debit()`\n  - `credit()`\n  - `can_launch()`\n  - `can_remediate()`\n\n## 2. Pipeline/gate system\n\n### Main modules\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/models.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py`\n\n### Generic pipeline executor\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py`:\n- `execute_pipeline()`\n- `_execute_single_step()`\n- `_run_parallel_steps()`\n- `_build_state()`\n\n#### `execute_pipeline()`\nThis is a reusable generic step sequencer:\n- accepts `list[Step | list[Step]]`\n- supports sequential steps and parallel step groups\n- delegates actual execution to injected `run_step`\n- invokes callbacks:\n  - `on_step_start`\n  - `on_step_complete`\n  - `on_state_update`\n- creates a `TrailingGateRunner` automatically if `config.grace_period > 0`\n- waits for trailing results at the end via `wait_for_pending()`\n\n#### `_execute_single_step()`\nCore behavior:\n- retry loop based on `retry_limit`\n- if no gate: trust `StepRunner` result\n- if timeout/cancelled: skip gate checking\n- if effective mode is trailing: submit to `TrailingGateRunner`, mark step PASS immediately\n- if blocking: run `gate_passed()` synchronously\n- if blocking gate fails and retries remain: retry\n- else return FAIL\n\n### Gate evaluation\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py`:\n- `gate_passed()`\n- `_check_frontmatter()`\n\n`gate_passed()` enforces increasing strictness by enforcement tier:\n- `EXEMPT`: always pass\n- `LIGHT`: file exists, non-empty\n- `STANDARD`: plus min line count and required frontmatter\n- `STRICT`: plus semantic checks\n\n### Data model\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/models.py`:\n- `Step`\n- `StepResult`\n- `PipelineConfig`\n- `GateCriteria`\n- `GateMode`\n- `SemanticCheck`\n- `StepStatus`\n\nImportant fields:\n- `Step.id`\n- `Step.prompt`\n- `Step.output_file`\n- `Step.gate`\n- `Step.retry_limit`\n- `Step.gate_mode`\n- `PipelineConfig.grace_period`\n\n### How sprint and pipeline relate\nThere are two overlapping architectures:\n\n1. Current concrete sprint runtime:\n- `execute_sprint()` launches per-phase Claude subprocesses directly.\n\n2. Generic pipeline/gate framework:\n- `execute_pipeline()` is intended as a reusable step engine with blocking/trailing gates.\n\nThere is evidence the repo is moving sprint toward consuming pipeline abstractions:\n- `SprintConfig` is described as inheriting shared pipeline fields\n- sprint imports `Step` and `StepResult`\n- sprint defines `SprintGatePolicy` as a concrete `TrailingGatePolicy`\n- tests cover sprint-facing gate features like display state, KPI reports, context injection, shadow mode\n\nBut in the code I inspected, `execute_sprint()` itself is not yet clearly wired through `execute_pipeline()`.\n\n## 3. TurnLedger: budget management\n\n### Main definition\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py`\n\n`TurnLedger` fields:\n- `initial_budget`\n- `consumed`\n- `reimbursed`\n- `reimbursement_rate = 0.5`\n- `minimum_allocation = 5`\n- `minimum_remediation_budget = 3`\n\nMethods:\n- `available()`: `initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed`\n- `debit(turns)`: increments `consumed`\n- `credit(turns)`: increments `reimbursed`\n- `can_launch()`: checks against `minimum_allocation`\n- `can_remediate()`: checks against `minimum_remediation_budget`\n\n### Where it is used\nPrimary sprint budget logic is in:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py`\n\n#### `check_budget_guard()`\nPreflight helper:\n- returns `None` if launch allowed\n- otherwise returns budget exhaustion message\n\n#### `execute_phase_tasks()`\nPer-task orchestration loop with economic accounting:\n- before each task:\n  - if no launch budget, mark remaining tasks skipped and stop\n- pre-debit `minimum_allocation`\n- run subprocess\n- classify task status from exit code:\n  - `0` -> PASS\n  - `124` -> INCOMPLETE\n  - other -> FAIL\n- reconcile preallocation against actual turn count:\n  - if actual > preallocated -> additional debit\n  - if actual < preallocated -> credit difference\n\nThis is the clearest concrete use of debit/credit logic in the sprint code.\n\n### Related budget/remediation semantics\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py`:\n- `attempt_remediation()` integrates with any budget source through injected callables:\n  - `can_remediate`\n  - `debit`\n\nThis makes remediation accounting pipeline-generic while still allowing sprint to use `TurnLedger`.\n\n## 4. Gate result queue and deferred remediation log\n\n### Gate result queue\nIn `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py`:\n- `TrailingGateResult`\n- `GateResultQueue`\n\n`GateResultQueue` is a thread-safe wrapper around stdlib `Queue`:\n- `put(result)`\n- `drain()`\n- `pending_count()`\n\nPurpose:\n- collect gate evaluation results from concurrent daemon threads\n- allow non-blocking draining by consumers\n\n### Trailing gate runner\nAlso in `trailing_gate.py`:\n- `TrailingGateRunner`\n\nKey methods:\n- `submit(step, gate_check=gate_passed)`\n- `drain()`\n- `wait_for_pending(timeout=30.0)`\n- `cancel()`\n- `pending_count`\n\nBehavior:\n- if `step.gate is None`, queue immediate pass result\n- otherwise spawn daemon thread\n- evaluate gate on `step.output_file`\n- record elapsed evaluation time in ms\n- push `TrailingGateResult` into queue\n- support cancellation and bounded waiting\n\n### Deferred remediation log\nIn the same file:\n- `DeferredRemediationLog`\n- `RemediationEntry`\n- `RemediationStatus`\n\nKey methods:\n- `append(gate_result)`\n- `pending_remediations()`\n- `mark_remediated(step_id)`\n- `serialize()`\n- `deserialize()`\n- `_write_to_disk()`\n- `load_from_disk(path)`\n- `entry_count`\n\nPurpose:\n- persist failed gate results that need remediation\n- support resume/recovery via JSON on disk\n- separate unresolved vs remediated items\n\n### Remediation helpers\nStill in `trailing_gate.py`:\n- `build_remediation_prompt()`\n- `attempt_remediation()`\n- `resolve_gate_mode()`\n\n#### `build_remediation_prompt()`\nBuilds a focused prompt including:\n- gate failure reason\n- original acceptance criteria\n- optional file paths\n- explicit instruction to fix only the gate issue\n\n#### `attempt_remediation()`\nTwo-attempt state machine:\n- check remediation budget\n- debit each attempt\n- run remediation step\n- re-check gate\n- return status:\n  - `PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT`\n  - `PASS_SECOND_ATTEMPT`\n  - `PERSISTENT_FAILURE`\n  - `BUDGET_EXHAUSTED`\n\n#### `resolve_gate_mode()`\nScope-based rules:\n- release gates: always blocking\n- milestone gates: configurable\n- task gates: trailing if `grace_period > 0`, else blocking\n\n## 5. Existing test structure\n\n## Sprint tests\nDirectory:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/`\n\nMost relevant files:\n- `test_executor.py`\n- `test_models.py`\n- `test_monitor.py`\n- `test_process.py`\n- `test_context_injection.py`\n- `test_gate_display_state.py`\n- `test_shadow_mode.py`\n- `test_kpi_report.py`\n\n### What sprint tests cover\n#### `test_executor.py`\nCovers:\n- `_determine_phase_status()` priority rules\n- integration behavior of `execute_sprint()`\n- budget guard\n- per-task subprocess orchestration\n- debit/credit reconciliation\n- isolation setup\n- aggregation to `AggregatedPhaseReport`\n- YAML/markdown report output expectations\n- backward compatibility defaults\n\n#### `test_models.py`\nCovers:\n- enums and dataclasses\n- `TurnLedger` thoroughly\n- `TaskResult.to_context_summary()`\n- `SprintConfig` path helpers\n- status classification properties\n\n#### `test_monitor.py`\nCovers:\n- regex extraction patterns\n- background polling\n- incremental reads\n- `detect_error_max_turns()`\n- `count_turns_from_output()`\n\n#### `test_process.py`\nCovers:\n- `ClaudeProcess.build_command()`\n- `build_env()`\n- `build_prompt()`\n- timeout math\n- OS fallback process behavior\n- signal handler install/uninstall\n- task context helpers\n\n#### `test_context_injection.py`\nVery detailed behavioral tests for:\n- single-task context\n- many-task context\n- progressive summarization\n- gate outcomes\n- remediation history\n\n#### `test_gate_display_state.py`\nCovers TUI-facing gate lifecycle modeling:\n- allowed transitions\n- enum values\n- display properties\n\n#### `test_shadow_mode.py`\nCovers:\n- config flag only\n- passive metrics collection via `ShadowGateMetrics`\n- explicit contract that shadow mode does not affect control flow\n\n#### `test_kpi_report.py`\nCovers:\n- `GateKPIReport`\n- aggregation from `TrailingGateResult`\n- integration with `DeferredRemediationLog`\n\n## Pipeline tests\nDirectory:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/pipeline/`\n\nMost relevant files:\n- `test_executor.py`\n- `test_trailing_gate.py`\n- `test_thread_safety.py`\n- `test_full_flow.py`\n- `test_gate_performance.py`\n- `conftest.py`\n\n### What pipeline tests cover\n#### `test_executor.py`\nCovers:\n- sequential step execution\n- no-gate behavior\n- retry on gate failure\n- pass on retry\n- callback order\n- halt-on-failure semantics\n- cancellation behavior\n- retry exhaustion\n\n#### `test_trailing_gate.py`\nCovers:\n- `GateResultQueue`\n- `TrailingGateRunner`\n- `DeferredRemediationLog`\n- gate mode resolution\n- executor branch behavior for blocking vs trailing\n- `TrailingGatePolicy` protocol conformance\n- remediation prompt construction\n- remediation retry logic\n\n#### `test_thread_safety.py`\nHeavy concurrency tests for:\n- queue correctness under concurrent put/drain\n- runner correctness under concurrent submit\n- step_id isolation\n- cancellation timing\n- repeated stability\n\n#### `test_full_flow.py`\nThis is the best architectural intent test.\nIt exercises scenario-level flows:\n1. gate pass -> continue\n2. gate fail -> remediation pass -> continue\n3. gate fail -> remediation fails twice -> HALT + diagnostics + resume output\n4. low budget -> remediation blocked -> HALT\n\nIt composes:\n- `TurnLedger`\n- `build_remediation_prompt()`\n- `attempt_remediation()`\n- `review_conflicts()`\n- `run_diagnostic_chain()`\n- `build_resume_output()`\n\n#### `test_gate_performance.py`\nNon-functional tests:\n- gate evaluation under 100KB input\n- latency population\n- deterministic behavior\n- no-gate fast path\n\n## Integration map\n\n### Sprint runtime path\n- CLI `run()` in `commands.py`\n  -> `load_sprint_config()` in `config.py`\n  -> `execute_sprint()` in `executor.py`\n  -> `ClaudeProcess` in `process.py`\n  -> subprocess output watched by `OutputMonitor` in `monitor.py`\n  -> `PhaseResult` / `SprintResult` stored with helpers in `models.py`\n\n### Task-level budget path\n- task inventory parsed by `parse_tasklist()`\n  -> `execute_phase_tasks()`\n  -> `TurnLedger.debit()/credit()/can_launch()`\n  -> `TaskResult`\n  -> `aggregate_task_results()`\n  -> `build_task_context()` for subsequent prompt context\n\n### Generic gate path\n- `Step` + `GateCriteria` in `pipeline/models.py`\n  -> `execute_pipeline()`\n  -> `_execute_single_step()`\n  -> either `gate_passed()` synchronously\n     or `TrailingGateRunner.submit()` asynchronously\n  -> trailing results collected via `GateResultQueue`\n  -> failures optionally captured in `DeferredRemediationLog`\n  -> remediation via `build_remediation_prompt()` + `attempt_remediation()`\n\n### Sprint-to-pipeline bridge points\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py`\n  imports:\n  - `Step`\n  - `StepResult`\n  - `TrailingGatePolicy`\n  - `TrailingGateResult`\n- `SprintGatePolicy` implements the pipeline-owned protocol\n\n### Observability path\n- gate results + remediation log\n  -> `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/kpi.py`\n  -> `GateKPIReport`\n\n## Mock patterns used in tests\n\nThere are very consistent patterns across both suites.\n\n### 1. Factory helpers instead of fixtures for domain objects\nCommon helper style:\n- `_make_config(...)`\n- `_make_step(...)`\n- `_make_gate(...)`\n- `_make_task_result(...)`\n- `_result(...)`\n\nThese keep tests short and local.\n\n### 2. `tmp_path` for nearly all file-based behavior\nTests heavily use pytest `tmp_path` to:\n- create output files\n- create result files\n- simulate sprint releases\n- validate persistence and reload\n\n### 3. In-memory runner lambdas/callables for orchestration\nPipeline tests usually avoid real subprocesses and instead inject callables:\n- `_make_runner(...)`\n- lambdas returning `StepResult`\n- custom retry runners with mutable counters\n\nThis is the dominant pipeline mocking pattern.\n\n### 4. `unittest.mock.patch` for subprocess and OS behavior\nSprint tests patch:\n- `subprocess.Popen`\n- `shutil.which`\n- OS capability checks like `hasattr(os, \"setpgrp\")`\n- process termination behavior\n\nExample pattern appears in:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_process.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_executor.py`\n\n### 5. Fake process objects instead of real subprocesses\nPattern:\n- tiny fake classes or `MagicMock` with:\n  - `poll()`\n  - `wait()`\n  - `returncode`\n  - `pid`\n\nUsed to drive:\n- pass\n- halt\n- timeout\n- interruption paths\n\n### 6. Real files, mocked orchestration boundaries\nEspecially in pipeline tests:\n- real file contents are written\n- real gate logic is exercised\n- orchestration boundary is mocked\n\nThis gives better confidence than pure unit mocks.\n\n### 7. Concurrency tests use real threads, not mocked threads\nThread-safety tests are real:\n- `threading.Thread`\n- `Barrier`\n- `Event`\n- small sleeps\n- repeated production/drain loops\n\nThis is important because the queue/runner semantics depend on real scheduling.\n\n## Existing test idioms worth noting\n\n- Tests are organized by behavior class, not by free-floating functions.\n- Many tests have contract-style names:\n  - “must produce”\n  - “does not affect”\n  - “halts”\n  - “reclassifies”\n- Regression tests are clearly called out in docstrings/comments.\n- There is good coverage of edge cases:\n  - case-insensitive result signals\n  - missing files\n  - malformed NDJSON\n  - timeout precedence\n  - exact threshold budgets\n  - cross-thread contamination\n\n## Important findings / caveats\n\n### 1. The gate system is well-developed as a generic framework\nThe pipeline side is coherent:\n- `Step`\n- `GateCriteria`\n- `execute_pipeline()`\n- trailing evaluation\n- remediation prompt/retry\n- persistence\n\n### 2. The sprint runner is only partially converged with that framework\nSprint has:\n- pipeline-shaped config/model overlap\n- a `SprintGatePolicy`\n- shadow gate metrics\n- KPI reporting based on gate results\n- tests for gate-related UI and observability\n\nBut the concrete `execute_sprint()` path I inspected still runs:\n- per-phase subprocesses\n- phase result parsing\n- direct monitor/TUI loop\n\nrather than obviously invoking `execute_pipeline()` end-to-end.\n\n### 3. `SprintGatePolicy.build_remediation_step()` currently creates directories\nArchitecturally, it’s the sprint-side implementation of the protocol, but in the code as written it does:\n- `output_dir.mkdir(...)`\n\nThat suggests this class is more forward-looking integration scaffolding than a purely passive adapter.\n\n### 4. `test_full_flow.py` is the best expression of intended system behavior\nIf you want the clearest single-file behavioral overview of how gates, budget, remediation, conflicts, diagnostics, and resume semantics are expected to work together, read:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/pipeline/test_full_flow.py`\n\n## Best files to read first if you want fast orientation\n\n1. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py`\n2. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py`\n3. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/process.py`\n4. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py`\n5. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/trailing_gate.py`\n6. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py`\n7. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/pipeline/test_full_flow.py`\n8. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/pipeline/test_trailing_gate.py`\n9. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_executor.py`\n10. `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_process.py`\n\n## Short answer by requested topic\n\n### Sprint runner architecture\n- Main execution is phase-by-phase.\n- `commands.py` -> `config.py` -> `executor.py` -> `process.py` + `monitor.py`.\n- Key classes: `SprintConfig`, `Phase`, `PhaseResult`, `SprintResult`, `ClaudeProcess`, `OutputMonitor`.\n\n### Pipeline/gate system\n- Generic step executor with blocking/trailing gate modes.\n- Key classes: `Step`, `StepResult`, `PipelineConfig`, `GateCriteria`, `TrailingGateRunner`, `GateResultQueue`, `DeferredRemediationLog`.\n- Main logic in `pipeline/executor.py`, `pipeline/gates.py`, `pipeline/trailing_gate.py`.\n\n### TurnLedger\n- Defined in sprint models.\n- Manages budget via `debit`, `credit`, `available`, `can_launch`, `can_remediate`.\n- Concrete use is strongest in `execute_phase_tasks()` and in test-composed remediation flows.\n\n### Gate result queue and deferred remediation log\n- Both live in `pipeline/trailing_gate.py`.\n- Queue is thread-safe collection for async gate results.\n- Remediation log is persistent JSON-backed state for unresolved gate failures.\n\n### Existing tests / mocks\n- Tests are extensive and behavior-focused.\n- Common patterns:\n  - `tmp_path`\n  - helper factories\n  - injected runner callables\n  - `patch()` on subprocess/OS boundaries\n  - fake process objects\n  - real-thread concurrency tests\n\nIf you want, I can do a second pass that turns this into a dependency graph tracking.\n   468→\n   469→    Tracks budget allocation, consumption, and reimbursement for sprint\n   470→    subprocesses. Enforces monotonicity: consumed can only increase.\n   471→    \"\"\"\n   472→\n   473→    initial_budget: int\n   474→    consumed: int = 0\n   475→    reimbursed: int = 0\n   476→    reimbursement_rate: float = 0.5\n   477→    minimum_allocation: int = 5\n   478→    minimum_remediation_budget: int = 3\n   479→\n   480→    def available(self) -> int:\n   481→        \"\"\"Return available turns: initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed.\"\"\"\n   482→        return self.initial_budget - self.consumed + self.reimbursed\n   483→\n   484→    def debit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n   485→        \"\"\"Consume turns from the budget. Enforces monotonicity.\"\"\"\n   486→        if turns < 0:\n   487→            raise ValueError(\"debit amount must be non-negative\")\n   488→        self.consumed += turns\n   489→\n   490→    def non-negative\")\n   494→        self.reimbursed += turns\n   495→\n   496→    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n   497→        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for a subprocess launch.\"\"\"\n   498→        return self.available() >= self.minimum_allocation\n   499→\n   500→    def can_remediate(self) -> bool:\n   501→        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for \"## HALT — Sprint Paused\",\n   535→        \"\",\n   536→        \"### Resume Command\",\n   537→        f\"```\",\n   538→        f\"superclaude sprint run {config.index_path} --resume resume: {budget_suggestion} turns\")\n   558→\n   559→    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n   560→\n   561→\n   562→@dataclass\n   563→class ShadowGateMetrics:\n   564→    \"\"\"Aggregated metrics from shadow gate evaluation.\n   565→\n   566→    Collected when ``--shadow-gates`` is enabled. Shadow metrics are\n   567→    informational only and do not affect sprint behavior.\n   568→    \"\"\"\n   569→\n   570→    total_evaluated: int = 0\n   571→    passed: int = 0\n   572→    failed: int = 0\n   573→    latency_ms: list[float] = field(default_factory=list)\n   574→\n   575→    @property\n   576→    def pass_rate(self) -> float:\n   577→        \"\"\"Fraction of gates that passed (0.0–1.0).\"\"\"\n   578→        if self.total_evaluated == 0:\n   579→            return 0.0\n   580→        return self.passed / self.total_evaluated\n   581→\n   582→    @property\n   583→    def p50_latency_ms(self) -> float:\n   584→        \"\"\"Median gate evaluation latency in milliseconds.\"\"\"\n   585→        if not self.latency_ms:\n   586→            return 0.0\n   587→        s = sorted(self.latency_ms)\n   588→        mid = len(s) // 2\n   589→        if len(s) % 2 == 0:\n   590→            return (s[mid - 1] + s[mid]) / 2\n   591→        return s[mid]\n   592→\n   593→    @property\n   594→    def p95_latency_ms(self) -> float:\n   595→        \"\"\"95th percentile gate evaluation latency in milliseconds.\"\"\"\n   596→        if not self.latency_ms:\n   597→            return 0.0\n   598→        s = sorted(self.latency_ms)\n   599→        idx = int(len(s) * 0.95)\n   600→        return s[min(idx, len(s) - 1)]\n   601→\n   602→    def record(self, passed: bool, evaluation_ms: float) -> None:\n   603→        \"\"\"Record a single shadow gate result.\"\"\"\n   604→        self.total_evaluated += 1\n   605→        if passed:\n   606→            self.passed += 1\n   607→        else:\n   608→            self.failed += 1\n   609→        self.latency_ms.append(evaluation_ms)\n   610→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"70dbfee0-5fa3-4d5b-b49a-0e95948cd141","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py","content":"\"\"\"Sprint data models — enums, dataclasses, and pure-data types.\n\nAll other sprint modules depend on these types. passed=passed,\n   144→                    evaluation_ms=elapsed_ms,\n   145→                    failure_reason=reason,\n   146→                )\n   147→                self._queue.put(result)\n   148→                _log.debug(\n   149→                    \"Trailing gate for '%s': %s (%.1fms)\",\n   150→                    step.id,\n   151→                    \"PASS\" if passed else \"FAIL\",\n   152→                    elapsed_ms,\n   153→                )\n   154→            except Exception:\n   155→                _log.exception(\"Trailing gate evaluation error for step '%s'\", step.id)\n   156→                self._queue.put(\n   157→                    TrailingGateResult(\n   158→                        step_id=step.id,\n   159→                        passed=False,\n   160→                        evaluation_ms=0.0,\n   161→                        failure_reason=\"Gate evaluation raised an exception\",\n   162→                    )\n   163→                )\n   164→            finally:\n   165→                with self._lock:\n   166→                    self._pending_count -= 1\n   167→\n   168→        t = threading.Thread(target=_evaluate, daemon=True)\n   169→        self._threads.append(t)\n   170→        t.start()\n   171→\n   172→    def drain(self) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n   173→        \"\"\"Collect all completed gate results.\"\"\"\n   174→        return self._queue.drain()\n   175→\n   176→    def wait_for_pending(self, timeout: float = 30.0) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n   177→        \"\"\"Block until all pending gate evaluations complete or timeout.\n   178→\n   179→        Returns all accumulated results. Never hangs indefinitely.\n   180→        \"\"\"\n   181→        deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout\n   182→        # Join all active threads with bounded timeout\n   183→        for t in list(self._threads):\n   184→            remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()\n   185→            if remaining <= 0:\n   186→                _log.warning(\"wait_for_pending timed out with %d threads still active\", self._pending_count)\n   187→                break\n   188→            t.join(timeout=remaining)\n   189→\n   190→        # Drain whatever results are available\n   191→        return self.drain()\n   192→\n   193→    def cancel(self) -> None:\n   194→        \"\"\"Signal cancellation to all pending daemon threads.\n   195→\n   196→        Threads check the cancellation event and terminate gracefully.\n   197→        \"\"\"\n   198→        self._cancelled.set()\n   199→        # Give threads a brief window to notice cancellation\n   200→        for t in \"\"\"Number of gate evaluations still in progress.\"\"\"\n   207→        with self._lock:\n   208→            return self._pending_count\n   209→\n   210→\n   211→# 336→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   337→\n   338→\n   339→class RemediationRetryStatus(Enum):\n   340→    \"\"\"Outcome of a remediation retry semantics and budget integration.\n   367→\n   368→    State machine:\n   369→    - Pre-check: can_remediate() → False → BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\n   370→    - ATTEMPT_1: debit → run → gate → pass → 373→    - ATTEMPT_2 fail: PERSISTENT_FAILURE (both attempts debited)\n   374→\n   375→    Both attempts' turns are consumed on persistent failure (no reimbursement).\n   376→\n   377→    Args:\n   378→        remediation_step: The Step to execute for remediation.\n   379→        turns_per_attempt: Turns to debit per attempt.\n   380→        can_remediate: Callable returning True if budget allows remediation.\n   381→        debit: Callable to consume turns from budget.\n   382→        run_step: Callable to execute the remediation step.\n   383→        check_gate: Callable to evaluate gate on step result.\n   384→\n   385→    Returns:\n   386→        RemediationRetryResult with status and accounting.\n   387→    \"\"\"\n   388→    total_turns = 0\n   389→\n   390→    # Pre-check: enough budget for first attempt?\n   391→    if not can_remediate():\n   392→        return RemediationRetryResult(\n   393→            status=RemediationRetryStatus.BUDGET_EXHAUSTED,\n   394→            attempts_made=0,\n   395→            turns_consumed=0,\n   396→        )\n   397→\n   398→    # --- Attempt 1 ---\n   399→    debit(turns_per_attempt)\n   400→    total_turns += turns_per_attempt\n   401→    result_1 = run_step(remediation_step)\n   402→    gate_1 = check_gate(result_1)\n   403→\n   404→    if gate_1.passed:\n   405→        return RemediationRetryResult(\n   406→            status=RemediationRetryStatus.PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT,\n   407→            attempts_made=1,\n   408→            turns_consumed=total_turns,\n   409→            final_gate_result=gate_1,\n   410→        )\n   411→\n   412→    _log.info(\n   413→        \"Remediation attempt 1 for '%s' failed: %s\",\n   414→        remediation_step.id, gate_1.failure_reason,\n   415→    )\n   416→\n   417→    # Pre-check attempts_made=1,\n   422→            turns_consumed=total_turns,\n   423→            final_gate_result=gate_1,\n   424→        )\n   425→\n   426→    # --- Attempt 2 ---\n   427→
