574→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   575→# T05.04 -- Scope-Based Gate Strategy\n   576→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   577→\n   578→\n   579→class GateScope(Enum):\n   580→    \"\"\"Scope levels for gate strategy resolution.\"\"\"\n   581→\n   582→    RELEASE = \"release\"\n   583→    MILESTONE = \"milestone\"\n   584→    TASK = \"task\"\n   585→\n   586→\n   587→def resolve_gate_mode(\n   588→    scope: GateScope,\n   589→    config_gate_mode: GateMode = GateMode.BLOCKING,\n   590→    grace_period: int = 0,\n   591→) -> GateMode:\n   592→    \"\"\"Resolve the effective gate mode based on scope and configuration.\n   593→\n   594→    Rules:\n   595→    - Release scope: ALWAYS BLOCKING (immutable, not configurable)\n   596→    - Milestone scope: configurable, defaults to BLOCKING\n   597→    - Task scope: TRAILING when grace_period > 0, else BLOCKING\n   598→\n   599→    Args:\n   600→        scope: The gate scope level.\n   601→        config_gate_mode: The configured gate mode (used for milestone scope).\n   602→        grace_period: Grace period in seconds (used for task scope).\n   603→\n   604→    Returns:\n   605→        The effective GateMode for this scope.\n   606→    \"\"\"\n   607→    if scope == GateScope.RELEASE:\n   608→        # Release gates are ALWAYS blocking -- enforced invariant\n   609→        return GateMode.BLOCKING\n   610→\n   611→    if scope == GateScope.MILESTONE:\n   612→        # Milestone gates default to BLOCKING but are configurable\n   613→        return config_gate_mode\n   614→\n   615→    if scope == GateScope.TASK:\n   616→        # Task gates trail when grace_period > 0\n   617→        if grace_period > 0:\n   618→            return GateMode.TRAILING\n   619→        return GateMode.BLOCKING\n   620→\n   621→    # Fallback: BLOCKING\n   622→    return gate infrastructure -- async gate evaluation with deferred remediation.\n\nProvides three components for non-blocking gate evaluation:\n- TrailingGateRunner: daemon-thread gate evaluator with submit/drain/wait/cancel\n- GateResultQueue: thread-safe result collection from concurrent gate evaluations\n- DeferredRemediationLog: persistent log of gate failures requiring remediation\n\nNFR-007: No imports from superclaude.cli.sprint or superclaude.cli.roadmap.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom queue import Empty, Queue\nfrom typing import Callable, Protocol, runtime_checkable\n\nfrom .gates import gate_passed\nfrom .models import GateCriteria, GateMode, Step, StepResult\n\n_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.pipeline.trailing_gate\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T05.02 -- GateResultQueue\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TrailingGateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of a trailing gate evaluation.\"\"\"\n\n    step_id: str\n    passed: bool\n    evaluation_ms: float\n    failure_reason: str | None = None\n\n\nclass GateResultQueue:\n    \"\"\"Thread-safe queue for collecting gate evaluation results.\n\n    Wraps stdlib ``queue.Queue`` with typed put/drain/pending_count methods.\n    Safe for concurrent access from multiple daemon threads.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._queue: Queue[TrailingGateResult] = Queue()\n        self._pending = 0\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def put(self, result: TrailingGateResult) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Enqueue a gate result. Thread-safe.\"\"\"\n        self._queue.put(result)\n        with self._lock:\n            self._pending += 1\n\n    def drain(self) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n        \"\"\"Collect all available results without blocking.\n\n        Returns a list of all results currently in the queue.\n        \"\"\"\n        results: list[TrailingGateResult] = []\n        while True:\n            try:\n                result = self._queue.get_nowait()\n                results.append(result)\n            except Empty:\n                break\n        with self._lock:\n            self._pending = max(0, self._pending - len(results))\n        return results\n\n    def pending_count(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Return count of unprocessed results.\"\"\"\n        return self._queue.qsize()\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T05.01 -- TrailingGateRunner\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TrailingGateRunner:\n    \"\"\"Daemon-thread gate evaluator for trailing gate mode.\n\n    Spawns daemon threads to evaluate gate criteria on step output files\n    after the step subprocess completes. Results are collected via the\n    internal GateResultQueue.\n\n    Thread safety: submit() and drain() may be called concurrently from\n    any thread. cancel() propagates to all active daemon threads.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, result_queue: GateResultQueue | None = None) -> None:\n        self._queue = result_queue or GateResultQueue()\n        self._threads: list[threading.Thread] = []\n        self._cancelled = threading.Event()\n        self._pending_count = 0\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    @property\n    def result_queue(self) -> GateResultQueue:\n        return self._queue\n\n    def submit(\n        self,\n        step: Step,\n        gate_check: Callable[[Path, GateCriteria], tuple[bool, str | None]] = gate_passed,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Spawn a daemon thread to evaluate gate criteria on step output.\n\n        The thread terminates after evaluation completes or cancellation.\n        \"\"\"\n        if step.gate is None:\n            # No gate to evaluate; submit a pass result immediately\n            self._queue.put(\n                TrailingGateResult(\n                    step_id=step.id,\n                    passed=True,\n                    evaluation_ms=0.0,\n                    failure_reason=None,\n                )\n            )\n            return\n\n        with self._lock:\n            self._pending_count += 1\n\n        def _evaluate() -> None:\n            try:\n                if self._cancelled.is_set():\n                    return\n                start = time.monotonic()\n                passed, reason = gate_check(step.output_file, step.gate)\n                elapsed_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n                result = TrailingGateResult(\n                    step_id=step.id,\n                    passed=passed,\n                    evaluation_ms=elapsed_ms,\n                    failure_reason=reason,\n                )\n                self._queue.put(result)\n                _log.debug(\n                    \"Trailing gate for '%s': %s (%.1fms)\",\n                    step.id,\n                    \"PASS\" if passed else \"FAIL\",\n                    elapsed_ms,\n                )\n            except Exception:\n                _log.exception(\"Trailing gate evaluation error for step '%s'\", step.id)\n                self._queue.put(\n                    TrailingGateResult(\n                        step_id=step.id,\n                        passed=False,\n                        evaluation_ms=0.0,\n                        failure_reason=\"Gate evaluation raised an exception\",\n                    )\n                )\n            finally:\n                with self._lock:\n                    self._pending_count -= 1\n\n        t = threading.Thread(target=_evaluate, daemon=True)\n        self._threads.append(t)\n        t.start()\n\n    def drain(self) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n        \"\"\"Collect all completed gate results.\"\"\"\n        return self._queue.drain()\n\n    def wait_for_pending(self, timeout: float = 30.0) -> list[TrailingGateResult]:\n        \"\"\"Block until all pending gate evaluations complete or timeout.\n\n        Returns all accumulated results. Never hangs indefinitely.\n        \"\"\"\n        deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout\n        # Join all active threads with bounded timeout\n        for t in list(self._threads):\n            remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()\n            if remaining <= 0:\n                _log.warning(\"wait_for_pending timed out with %d threads still active\", self._pending_count)\n                break\n            t.join(timeout=remaining)\n\n        # Drain whatever results are available\n        return self.drain()\n\n    def cancel(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Signal cancellation to all pending daemon threads.\n\n        Threads check the cancellation event and terminate gracefully.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cancelled.set()\n        # Give threads a brief window to notice cancellation\n        for t in list(self._threads):\n            t.join(timeout=1.0)\n        self._threads.clear()\n\n    @property\n    def pending_count(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Number of gate evaluations still in progress.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            return self._pending_count\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T05.03 -- DeferredRemediationLog\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T07.01 -- TrailingGatePolicy Protocol\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@runtime_checkable\nclass TrailingGatePolicy(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Consumer-owned hooks for remediation step construction and file tracking.\n\n    The sprint runner implements this protocol to build remediation steps\n    and detect file changes, decoupling gate infrastructure from sprint-specific\n    logic.\n\n    Methods:\n        build_remediation_step: Construct a focused remediation Step from a gate failure.\n        files_changed: Return the set of file paths modified by a step execution.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def build_remediation_step(self, gate_result: TrailingGateResult) -> Step:\n        \"\"\"Build a remediation Step targeting the specific gate failure.\n\n        Args:\n            gate_result: The failed gate evaluation result.\n\n        Returns:\n            A Step configured to remediate the specific failure.\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def files_changed(self, step_result: StepResult) -> set[Path]:\n        \"\"\"Return file paths modified during step execution.\n\n        Args:\n            step_result: The result of executing a step.\n\n        Returns:\n            Set of Path objects for files changed by the step.\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T07.02 -- Remediation Prompt Construction\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef build_remediation_prompt(\n    gate_result: TrailingGateResult,\n    original_step: Step,\n    file_paths: set[Path] | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build a focused remediation prompt from gate failure data.\n\n    Assembles a subprocess prompt containing:\n    - Gate failure reason (specific to the failure)\n    - Original acceptance criteria (from the step's gate)\n    - File paths involved in the failure\n    - Remediation-specific instructions\n\n    The prompt is scoped to the specific failure, not a re-execution\n    of the full task.\n\n    Args:\n        gate_result: The failed gate evaluation result.\n        original_step: The original Step that failed the gate.\n        file_paths: Optional set of file paths involved in the failure.\n\n    Returns:\n        A deterministic remediation prompt string.\n    \"\"\"\n    sections: list[str] = []\n\n    # Header\n    sections.append(\n        f\"# Remediation for step '{gate_result.step_id}'\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Failure reason\n    reason = gate_result.failure_reason or \"Unknown gate failure\"\n    sections.append(\n        f\"## Gate Failure\\n{reason}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Original acceptance criteria\n    if original_step.gate is not None:\n        gate = original_step.gate\n        criteria_lines = [\n            f\"- Required frontmatter fields: {', '.join(gate.required_frontmatter_fields)}\",\n            f\"- Minimum lines: {gate.min_lines}\",\n            f\"- Enforcement tier: {gate.enforcement_tier}\",\n        ]\n        if gate.semantic_checks:\n            for check in gate.semantic_checks:\n                criteria_lines.append(f\"- Semantic check: {check.name}\")\n        sections.append(\n            \"## Acceptance Criteria\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(criteria_lines) + \"\\n\"\n        )\n\n    # File paths\n    if file_paths:\n        path_lines = [f\"- {p}\" for p in sorted(file_paths)]\n        sections.append(\n            \"## Files Involved\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(path_lines) + \"\\n\"\n        )\n\n    # Instructions\n    sections.append(\n        \"## Instructions\\n\"\n        \"Fix ONLY the specific gate failure described above.\\n\"\n        \"Do not re-execute the full task. Focus on making the output \"\n        \"satisfy the acceptance criteria.\\n\"\n        f\"Output file: {original_step.output_file}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(sections)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T07.03 -- Remediation Retry with TurnLedger Integration\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass RemediationRetryStatus(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Outcome of a remediation retry sequence.\"\"\"\n\n    PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT = \"pass_first_attempt\"\n    PASS_SECOND_ATTEMPT = \"pass_second_attempt\"\n    PERSISTENT_FAILURE = \"persistent_failure\"\n    BUDGET_EXHAUSTED = \"budget_exhausted\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RemediationRetryResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of a remediation retry sequence.\"\"\"\n\n    status: RemediationRetryStatus\n    attempts_made: int\n    turns_consumed: int\n    final_gate_result: run_step: Callable[[Step], StepResult],\n    check_gate: Callable[[StepResult], TrailingGateResult],\n) -> RemediationRetryResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute remediation with retry-once semantics and budget integration.\n\n    State machine:\n    - Pre-check: can_remediate() → False → BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\n    - ATTEMPT_1: debit → run → gate → pass → PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT\n    - ATTEMPT_1 fail: can_remediate() check → False → PERSISTENT_FAILURE (1 attempt debited)\n    - ATTEMPT_2: debit → run → gate → pass → PASS_SECOND_ATTEMPT\n    - ATTEMPT_2 fail: PERSISTENT_FAILURE (both attempts debited)\n\n    Both attempts' turns are consumed on persistent failure (no reimbursement).\n\n    Args:\n        remediation_step: The Step to execute for remediation.\n        turns_per_attempt: Turns to debit per attempt.\n        can_remediate: Callable returning True if budget allows remediation.\n        debit: Callable to consume turns from budget.\n        run_step: Callable to execute the remediation step.\n        check_gate: Callable to evaluate gate on step result.\n\n    Returns:\n        RemediationRetryResult with status and accounting.\n    \"\"\"\n    total_turns = 0\n\n    # Pre-check: enough budget for first attempt?\n    if not can_remediate():\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.BUDGET_EXHAUSTED,\n            attempts_made=0,\n            turns_consumed=0,\n        )\n\n    # --- Attempt 1 ---\n    debit(turns_per_attempt)\n    total_turns += turns_per_attempt\n    result_1 = run_step(remediation_step)\n    gate_1 = check_gate(result_1)\n\n    if gate_1.passed:\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT,\n            attempts_made=1,\n            turns_consumed=total_turns,\n            final_gate_result=gate_1,\n        )\n\n    _log.info(\n        \"Remediation attempt 1 for '%s' failed: %s\",\n        remediation_step.id, gate_1.failure_reason,\n    )\n\n    # Pre-check before attempt 2\n    if not can_remediate():\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.PERSISTENT_FAILURE,\n            attempts_made=1,\n            turns_consumed=total_turns,\n            final_gate_result=gate_1,\n        )\n\n    # --- Attempt 2 ---\n    debit(turns_per_attempt)\n    total_turns += turns_per_attempt\n    result_2 = run_step(remediation_step)\n    gate_2 = check_gate(result_2)\n\n    if gate_2.passed:\n        return RemediationRetryResult(\n            status=RemediationRetryStatus.PASS_SECOND_ATTEMPT,\n            attempts_made=2,\n            turns_consumed=total_turns,\n            final_gate_result=gate_2,\n        )\n\n    _log.info(\n        \"Remediation attempt 2 for '%s' failed (persistent): %s\",\n        remediation_step.id, gate_2.failure_reason,\n    )\n\n    # Persistent failure: both attempts' turns lost\n    return RemediationRetryResult(\n        status=RemediationRetryStatus.PERSISTENT_FAILURE,\n        attempts_made=2,\n        turns_consumed=total_turns,\n        final_gate_result=gate_2,\n    )\n\n\nclass remediation_status=data.get(\"remediation_status\", RemediationStatus.PENDING.value),\n        )\n\n\nclass DeferredRemediationLog:\n    \"\"\"Persistent log of gate failures requiring remediation.\n\n    Single-writer thread safety: main thread writes, daemon threads may read.\n    Supports disk persistence for --resume recovery.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, persist_path: TrailingGateResult) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Add a remediation entry from a failed gate result.\"\"\"\n        entry = RemediationEntry(\n            step_id=gate_result.step_id,\n self._entries.append(entry)\n        if self._persist_path:\n            self._write_to_disk()\n\n    def pending_remediations(self) -> list[RemediationEntry]:\n        \"\"\"Return all unresolved remediation entries.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            return [\n                e for e in self._entries\n                if e.remediation_status == RemediationStatus.PENDING.value\n            ]\n\n    def mark_remediated(self, step_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Mark a remediation entry as resolved. RemediationStatus.PENDING.value:\n                    entry.remediation_status = RemediationStatus.REMEDIATED.value\n                    break\n            else:\n                return False\n        if self._persist_path:\n            self._write_to_disk()\n        return True\n\n    def serialize(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Serialize to JSON string for disk persistence.\"\"\"\n        with None) -> DeferredRemediationLog:\n        \"\"\"Recover state from JSON string for --resume support.\"\"\"\n        data = json.loads(json_str)\n        log = cls(persist_path=persist_path)\n        log._entries = [RemediationEntry.from_dict(d) for d in data]\n        return log\n\n    def _write_to_disk(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist current state to disk.\"\"\"\n        if  @classmethod\n    def load_from_disk(cls, path: Path) -> DeferredRemediationLog:\n        \"\"\"Load from disk if file exists, otherwise return empty log.\"\"\"\n        if path.exists():\n int:\n        with self._lock:\n            return len(self._entries)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T05.04 -- Scope-Based Gate Strategy\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass GateScope(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Scope levels for gate strategy resolution.\"\"\"\n\n    RELEASE = \"release\"\n    MILESTONE = \"milestone\"\n    TASK = \"task\"\n\n\ndef resolve_gate_mode(\n    scope: GateScope,\n    config_gate_mode: GateMode = GateMode.BLOCKING,\n    grace_period: int = 0,\n) -> GateMode:\n    \"\"\"Resolve the effective gate mode based on scope and configuration.\n\n    Rules:\n    - Release scope: ALWAYS BLOCKING (immutable, not configurable)\n    - Milestone scope: configurable, defaults to BLOCKING\n    - Task scope: TRAILING when grace_period > 0, else BLOCKING\n\n    Args:\n        scope: The gate scope level.\n        config_gate_mode: The configured gate mode (used for milestone scope).\n        grace_period: Grace period in seconds (used for task scope).\n\n    Returns:\n        The effective GateMode for this scope.\n    \"\"\"\n    if scope == GateScope.RELEASE:\n        # Release gates are ALWAYS blocking -- enforced invariant\n        return GateMode.BLOCKING\n\n    if scope == GateScope.MILESTONE:\n        # Milestone gates default to BLOCKING but are configurable\n        return config_gate_mode\n\n    if scope == GateScope.TASK:\n        # Task gates trail when grace_period > 0\n        if grace_period > 0:\n            return GateMode.TRAILING\n        return Frontmatter is delimited by --- at start and end\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter missing or unparseable in {output_file}: no opening ---\"\n\n    # Find closing ---\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter missing or unparseable in {output_file}: no closing ---\"\n\n    frontmatter_text = rest[:end_idx]\n\n    # Simple key extraction (key: value lines)\n    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n                found_keys.add(key)\n\n    for field in required_fields:\n        if field not in found_keys:\n            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n\n    return True, None\n","numLines":102,"startLine":1,"totalLines":102}}}

report.tasks_failed = sum(\n   316→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n   317→    )\n   318→    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n   319→        1 for r in task_results if report.tasks_not_attempted = len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n   325→    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n   326→    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(\n   327→        r.duration_seconds for r in task_results\n   328→    )\n   329→\n   330→    return report\n   331→\n   332→\n   333→def check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n   334→    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n   335→\n   336→    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n   337→    if the 349→def execute_phase_tasks(\n   350→    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n   351→    config: SprintConfig,\n   352→    phase,\n   353→    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n   354→    *,\n   355→    _subprocess_factory=None,\n   356→) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n   357→    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n   358→\n   359→    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n   360→    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n   361→    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n   362→\n   363→    Args:\n   364→        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n   365→        config: Sprint configuration.\n   366→        phase: The Phase being executed.\n   367→        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n   368→        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n   369→            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n   370→\n   371→    Returns:\n   372→        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n   373→        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n   374→    \"\"\"\n   375→    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n   376→    remaining: list[str] = []\n   377→\n   378→    if not tasks:\n   379→        return results, remaining\n   380→\n   381→    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n   382→        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   383→\n   384→        # Budget check: can we launch?\n   385→        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n   386→            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n   387→            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n   388→            for t in tasks[i:]:\n   389→                results.append(\n   390→                    TaskResult(\n   391→                        task=t,\n   392→                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n   393→                        started_at=started_at,\n   394→                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   395→                    )\n   396→                )\n   397→            break\n   398→\n   399→        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n   400→        if ledger is not None:\n   401→            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n   402→\n   403→        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n   404→        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n   405→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n   406→                task, config, phase\n   407→            )\n   408→        else:\n   409→            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n   410→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n   411→                task, config, phase\n   412→            )\n   413→\n   414→        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   415→\n   416→        # Determine task status from exit code\n   417→        if exit_code == 0:\n   418→            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n   419→        elif exit_code == 124:\n   420→            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   421→        else:\n   422→            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n   423→\n   424→        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n   425→        if ledger is not None:\n   426→            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n   427→            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n   428→            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n   429→            if actual > pre_allocated:\n   430→                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n   431→            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n   432→                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n   433→\n   434→        results.append(\n   435→            TaskResult(\n   436→                task=task,\n   437→                status=status,\n   438→                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n   439→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   440→                started_at=started_at,\n   441→                finished_at=finished_at,\n   442→                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n   443→            )\n   444→        )\n   445→\n   446→    return results, remaining\n   447→\n   448→\n   449→def _run_task_subprocess(\n   450→    task: TaskEntry,\n   451→    config: SprintConfig,\n   452→    phase,\n   453→) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n   454→    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n   455→\n   456→    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n   457→    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n   458→    \"\"\"\n   459→    # Build a task-specific prompt\n   460→    prompt = (\n   461→        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n   462→        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n   463→        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n   464→    )\n   465→\n   466→    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n   467→    proc.config = config\n   468→    proc.phase = phase\n   469→    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n   470→    _Base.__init__(\n   471→        proc,\n   472→        prompt=prompt,\n   473→        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n   474→        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n   475→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   476→        model=config.model,\n   477→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   478→        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n   479→        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n   480→    )\n   481→    proc.start()\n   482→    proc.wait()\n   483→    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n   484→    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   485→    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n   486→    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n   487→    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n   488→\n   489→\n   490→def execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n   491→    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n   492→\n   493→    For each active phase:\n   494→    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n   495→    2. Start output monitor thread\n   496→    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n   497→    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n   498→    5. Record PhaseResult\n   499→    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n   500→    \"\"\"\n   501→    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n   502→    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n   503→        raise SystemExit(\n   504→            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n   505→            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n   506→        )\n   507→\n   508→    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   509→    signal_handler.install()\n   510→\n   511→    setup_debug_logger(config)\n   512→    import logging as _logging\n   513→    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n   514→\n   515→    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n   516→    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n   517→    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n   518→    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n   519→\n   520→    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n   521→    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n   522→\n   523→    tui.start()\n   524→\n   525→    try:\n   526→        for phase in config.active_phases:\n   527→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   528→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   529→                break\n   530→\n   531→            # Reset monitor for this phase\n   532→            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   533→            monitor.reset(output_path)\n   534→            monitor.start()\n   535→\n   536→            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n   537→            if config.tmux_session_name:\n   538→                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n   539→\n   540→            # Launch claude\n   541→            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n   542→            proc_manager.start()\n   543→            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   544→            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n   545→            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n   546→            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n   547→\n   548→            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n   549→\n   550→            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   551→\n   552→            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n   553→            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n   554→            _timed_out = False\n   555→            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n   556→            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n   557→            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n   558→                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   559→                    proc_manager.terminate()\n   560→                    break\n   561→                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n   562→                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n   563→                    _timed_out = True\n   564→                    proc_manager.terminate()\n   565→                    break\n   566→\n   567→                ms = monitor.state\n   568→                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n   569→\n   570→                debug_log(\n   571→                    _dbg,\n   572→                    \"poll_tick\",\n   573→                    phase=phase.number,\n   574→                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   575→                    poll_result=\"running\",\n   576→                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n   577→                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n   578→                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n   579→                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   580→                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n   581→                )\n   582→\n   583→                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n   584→                if (\n   585→                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n   586→                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n   587→                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n   588→                    and not _stall_acted\n   589→                ):\n   590→                    _stall_acted = True\n   591→                    debug_log(\n   592→                        _dbg,\n   593→                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n   594→                        phase=phase.number,\n   595→                        action=config.stall_action,\n   596→                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   597→                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   598→                    )\n   599→                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n   600→                        import sys\n   601→                        print(\n   602→                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   603→                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n   604→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   605→                        )\n   606→                        _timed_out = True\n   607→                        proc_manager.terminate()\n   608→                        break\n   609→                    else:\n   610→                        # warn action: log and continue\n   611→                        import sys\n   612→                        print(\n   613→                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   614→                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n   615→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   616→                        )\n   617→\n   618→                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n   619→                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n   620→                    _stall_acted = False\n   621→\n   622→                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n   623→                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n   624→                try:\n   625→                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   626→                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n   627→                    import sys\n   628→                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n   629→                time.sleep(0.5)\n   630→\n   631→            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n   632→            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n   633→            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n   634→            if _timed_out:\n   635→                exit_code = 124\n   636→            else:\n   637→                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n   638→            monitor.stop()\n   639→            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   640→            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   641→            debug_log(\n   642→                _dbg,\n   643→                \"PHASE_END\",\n   644→                phase=phase.number,\n   645→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   646→                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   647→            )\n   648→\n   649→            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n   650→            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n   651→            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n   652→            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n   653→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   654→                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n   655→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   656→                break\n   657→\n   658→            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n   666→            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n   667→            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n   668→\n   669→            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n   670→                phase=phase,\n   671→                status=status,\n   672→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   673→                started_at=started_at,\n   674→                finished_at=finished_at,\n   675→                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n   676→                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n   677→                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n   678→                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n   679→            )\n   680→            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n   681→\n   682→            debug_log(\n   683→                _dbg,\n   684→                \"phase_complete\",\n   685→                phase=phase.number,\n   686→                status=status.value,\n   687→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   688→                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   689→            )\n   690→\n   691→            # Decide: continue or halt?\n   698→            if status.is_failure:\n   699→                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n   700→                try:\n   701→                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n   702→                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n   703→                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n   704→                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n   705→                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n   706→                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n   707→                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n   708→                    debug_log(\n   709→                        _dbg,\n   710→                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n   711→                        phase=phase.number,\n   712→                        category=bundle.category.value,\n   713→                        path=str(diag_path),\n   714→                    )\n   715→                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n   716→                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n   717→\n   718→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n   719→                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n   720→                break\n   721→\n   722→        # Sprint finished\n   723→        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   724→        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n   725→            # Verify all phases actually passed\n   726→            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n   727→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n   728→\n   729→        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n   730→        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n   731→        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n   732→\n   733→    finally:\n   734→        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n   735→        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n   736→        try:\n   737→            monitor.stop()\n   738→        except Exception:\n   739→            pass\n   740→        if proc_manager is not None:\n   741→            try:\n   742→                proc_manager.terminate()\n   743→            except Exception:\n   744→                pass\n   745→        try:\n   746→            tui.stop()\n   747→        except Exception:\n   748→            pass\n   749→        try:\n   750→            signal_handler.uninstall()\n   751→        except Exception:\n   752→            pass\n   753→\n   754→    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n   755→    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n   756→    try:\n   757→        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n   758→    except OSError:\n   759→        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n   760→\n   761→    if _exitcode != 0:\n   762→        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n   763→\n   764→\n   765→def _determine_phase_status(\n   766→    exit_code: int,\n   767→    result_file: Path,\n   768→    output_file: Path,\n   769→) -> PhaseStatus:\n   770→    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n   771→\n   772→    Priority:\n   773→    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n   774→    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n   775→    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n   776→    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n   777→    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n   778→    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n   779→    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n   780→    \"\"\"\n   781→    if exit_code == 124:\n   782→        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n   783→    if exit_code != 0:\n   784→        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n   785→\n   786→    if result_file.exists():\n   787→        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n   788→        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n   789→        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n   790→        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n   791→        upper = content.upper()\n   792→        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n   793→        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n   794→        if has_halt:\n   795→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   796→        if has_continue:\n   797→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n   798→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   799→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n   800→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   801→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   802→        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n   803→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   804→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   805→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n   806→\n   807→    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n   808→        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n   809→        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n   810→        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n   811→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n   812→            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   813→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n   814→\n   815→    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n   816→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"85353454-e90a-4625-80ff-453d4e656d5e","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py","content":"\"\"\"Sprint def build_remediation_step(self, gate_result: TrailingGateResult) -> Step:\n        \"\"\"Build a focused remediation Step from a gate failure.\n\n        Constructs a Step whose prompt targets the specific failure reason\n        and acceptance criteria, rather than re-executing the entire task.\n        \"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateCriteria\n\n        prompt ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# 4-Layer Subprocess Isolation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Configuration for the 4-layer subprocess isolation.\n\n    Each layer prevents cross-task state leakage:\n    1. scoped_work_dir: Restrict working directory to release dir\n    2. git_boundary: Set GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to prevent upward traversal\n    3. empty_plugin_dir: Point CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR to an empty tempdir\n    4. restricted_settings: Set CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR to an isolated tempdir\n\n    The ``env_vars`` property returns a dict of environment variable overrides\n    that should be merged into the subprocess environment.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    scoped_work_dir: Path\n    git_boundary: Path\n    plugin_dir: Path\n    settings_dir: Path\n\n    @property\n    def env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Return environment variable overrides for all 4 isolation layers.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(self.scoped_work_dir),\n            \"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\": str(self.git_boundary),\n            \"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\": str(self.plugin_dir),\n            \"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\": str(self.settings_dir),\n        }\n\n    @property\n    def layers_active(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of active isolation layer names for verification.\"\"\"\n        active = []\n        if self.scoped_work_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"scoped_work_dir\")\n        if self.git_boundary.exists():\n            active.append(\"git_boundary\")\n        if self.plugin_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"empty_plugin_dir\")\n        if self.settings_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"restricted_settings\")\n        return active\n\n\ndef setup_isolation(config: SprintConfig) -> IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Create 4-layer isolation for subprocess execution.\n\n    Sets up:\n    1. Scoped working directory (the release dir)\n    2. Git boundary (prevents git operations above release dir)\n    3. Empty plugin directory (no plugins loaded)\n    4. Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n\n    All directories are created if they don't exist. The caller is\n    responsible for passing ``layers.env_vars`` to the subprocess.\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration providing the release directory.\n\n    Returns:\n        IsolationLayers with all 4 layers configured.\n    \"\"\"\n    base = config.results_dir / \".isolation\"\n    base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    plugin_dir = base / \"plugins\"\n    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    settings_dir = base / \"settings\"\n    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    return IsolationLayers(\n        scoped_work_dir=config.release_dir,\n Aggregation — runner-constructed phase reports\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Runner-constructed phase report from collected TaskResults.\n\n    This report is built by the runner, not by parsing agent self-reported\n    output, ensuring accurate task outcome tracking even when subprocesses\n    are budget-exhausted.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    phase_number: int\n    tasks_total: int = 0\n    tasks_passed: int = 0\n    tasks_failed: int = 0\n    tasks_incomplete: int = 0\n    tasks_skipped: int = 0\n    tasks_not_attempted: int = 0\n    budget_remaining: int = 0\n    total_turns_consumed: int = 0\n    total_duration_seconds: float = 0.0\n    task_results: list[TaskResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def status(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Overall phase status: PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL.\"\"\"\n        if self.tasks_total == 0:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == self.tasks_total:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == 0:\n            return \"FAIL\"\n        return \"PARTIAL\"\n\n    def to_yaml(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render the report as a YAML string.\n\n        Produces a machine-readable YAML document with standardized fields\n        for downstream tooling and TUI display.\n        \"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n            f\"tasks_incomplete: {self.tasks_incomplete}\",\n            f\"tasks_not_attempted: {self.tasks_not_attempted}\",\n            f\"budget_remaining: {self.budget_remaining}\",\n            f\"total_turns_consumed: {self.total_turns_consumed}\",\n            f\"total_duration_seconds: {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}\",\n            \"tasks:\",\n        ]\n        for tr in self.task_results:\n            lines.append(f\"  - task_id: {tr.task.task_id}\")\n            lines.append(f\"    title: \\\"{tr.task.title}\\\"\")\n            lines.append(f\"    status: {tr.status.value}\")\n            lines.append(f\"    gate_outcome: {tr.gate_outcome.value}\")\n            lines.append(f\"    turns_consumed: {tr.turns_consumed}\")\n            lines.append(f\"    duration_seconds: {tr.duration_seconds:.1f}\")\n        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n            lines.append(\"remaining_tasks:\")\n            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n                lines.append(f\"  - {tid}\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n\n    def to_markdown(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render the report as a markdown string with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            \"---\",\n            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n            \"---\",\n            \"\",\n            f\"# Phase {self.phase_number} — Aggregated Task Report\",\n |\"\n            )\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total turns consumed:** {self.total_turns_consumed}\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total duration:** {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}s\")\n\n        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Remaining Tasks (Budget Exhausted)\")\n            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n                lines.append(f\"- {tid}\")\n\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        if self.status == \"PASS\":\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n        else:\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n\n\ndef aggregate_task_results(\n    phase_number: int,\n    task_results: list[TaskResult],\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n    budget_remaining: int = 0,\n) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n\n    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n\n    Args:\n        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n\n    Returns:\n        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n    \"\"\"\n    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n        phase_number=phase_number,\n        task_results=task_results,\n        remaining_task_ids=remaining_task_ids or [],\n        budget_remaining=budget_remaining,\n    )\n\n    report.tasks_total = len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.tasks_passed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS\n    )\n    report.tasks_failed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n    )\n    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    )\n    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n    )\n    report.tasks_not_attempted = len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(\n        r.duration_seconds for r in task_results\n    )\n\n    return report\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_phase_tasks(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n    *,\n    _subprocess_factory=None,\n) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n\n    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Budget check: can we launch?\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\n                results.append(\n                    TaskResult(\n                        task=t,\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n                        started_at=started_at,\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n                    )\n                )\n            break\n\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n        if ledger is not None:\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n        else:\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Determine task status from exit code\n        if exit_code == 0:\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n        elif exit_code == 124:\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        else:\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n        if ledger is not None:\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n\n        results.append(\n            TaskResult(\n                task=task,\n                status=status,\n                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n            )\n        )\n\n    return results, remaining\n\n\ndef _run_task_subprocess(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n\n    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this phase\n            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n            if config.tmux_session_name:\n                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n            # Launch claude\n            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n            proc_manager.start()\n            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n            _timed_out = False\n            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                    _timed_out = True\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n\n                ms = monitor.state\n                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"poll_tick\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    poll_result=\"running\",\n                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                )\n\n                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                if (\n                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                    and not _stall_acted\n                ):\n                    _stall_acted = True\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        action=config.stall_action,\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    )\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    else:\n                        # warn action: log and continue\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n\n                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                    _stall_acted = False\n\n                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                try:\n                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                    import sys\n                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n            if _timed_out:\n                exit_code = 124\n            else:\n                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n            monitor.stop()\n            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"PHASE_END\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Determine phase status\n            status = _determine_phase_status(\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            )\n\n            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                phase=phase,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n            )\n            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"phase_complete\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                status=status.value,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # Log and notify\n            logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n            notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n            # Decide: continue or halt?\n            if status.is_failure:\n                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                try:\n                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        category=bundle.category.value,\n                        path=str(diag_path),\n                    )\n                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                break\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","numLines":816,"startLine":1,"totalLines":816}}}
