integration test -- 4 scenarios exercising the complete pipeline.\n\nCovers T07.07 acceptance criteria (Gap 6 deliverable: mandatory).\n\nScenarios:\n1. Task passes gate → continue to next task\n2. Task fails gate → remediation spawned → remediation passes → continue\n3. Task fails gate → remediation fails → retry fails → HALT with diagnostic\n4. Low budget → can_remediate() false → HALT with budget message\n\nMock boundaries: subprocess execution is mocked; real orchestration, budget,\ngate, remediation, conflict review, and diagnostic chain logic is exercised.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.conflict_review import (\n    ConflictAction,\n    review_conflicts,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.diagnostic_chain import (\n    DiagnosticStage,\n    run_diagnostic_chain,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import (\n    GateCriteria,\n    Step,\n    StepResult,\n    StepStatus,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.trailing_gate import (\n    RemediationRetryStatus,\n    TrailingGateResult,\n    attempt_remediation,\n    build_remediation_prompt,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.sprint.models import (\n    SprintConfig,\n    TaskEntry,\n    TurnLedger,\n    build_resume_output,\n)\n\n\ndef _make_step(step_id: str, tmp_path: Path, gate: GateCriteria | None = None) -> Step:\n    output = tmp_path / f\"{step_id}_output.md\"\n    return Step(\n        id=step_id, prompt=f\"Execute {step_id}\",\n        output_file=output, gate=gate, timeout_seconds=60,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_gate_result(step_id: str, passed: bool, reason: str | None = None):\n    return TrailingGateResult(\n        step_id=step_id, passed=passed,\n        evaluation_ms=10.0, failure_reason=reason,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_step_result(step: Step, status: StepStatus = StepStatus.PASS):\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    return StepResult(step=step, status=status, started_at=now, finished_at=now)\n\n\nclass TestFullFlowIntegration:\n    \"\"\"Full-flow integration test exercising 4 compound scenarios.\"\"\"\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Scenario 1: Task passes gate → continue\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def test_scenario_1_pass_gate_continue(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Task passes gate; budget debited and reimbursed; next task proceeds.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100)\n\n        gate = GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\"],\n            min_lines=3,\n            enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n        )\n        step = _make_step(\"task-1\", tmp_path, gate=gate)\n\n        # Simulate task execution\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        result = _make_step_result(step, StepStatus.PASS)\n\n        # Gate passes\n        gate_result = _make_gate_result(\"task-1\", passed=True)\n        assert gate_result.passed\n\n        # Reimbursement on pass\n        reimbursement = int(10 * ledger.reimbursement_rate)\n        ledger.credit(reimbursement)\n\n        # Budget accounting correct\n        assert ledger.consumed == 10\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == reimbursement\n        assert ledger.available() == 100 - 10 + reimbursement\n\n        # No remediation needed, no conflict review needed\n        # Next task can proceed\n        assert ledger.can_launch()\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Scenario 2: Task fails gate → remediation succeeds → continue\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def test_scenario_2_fail_remediate_pass(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Task fails gate, remediation passes on first attempt, continues.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_remediation_budget=5)\n\n        gate = GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\"],\n            min_lines=5,\n            enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n        )\n        step = _make_step(\"task-2\", tmp_path, gate=gate)\n\n        # Task execution\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        task_result = _make_step_result(step, StepStatus.PASS)\n\n        # Gate fails\n        gate_result = _make_gate_result(\n            \"task-2\", passed=False,\n            reason=\"Missing required field 'title'\",\n        )\n        assert not gate_result.passed\n\n        # Build remediation prompt\n        prompt = build_remediation_prompt(\n            gate_result, step,\n            file_paths={step.output_file},\n        )\n        assert \"Missing required field 'title'\" in prompt\n\n        # Build remediation step\n        remediation_step = _make_step(\"task-2_remediation\", tmp_path)\n\n        # Attempt remediation (passes first attempt)\n        retry_result = attempt_remediation(\n            remediation_step=remediation_step,\n            turns_per_attempt=5,\n            can_remediate=ledger.can_remediate,\n            debit=ledger.debit,\n            run_step=lambda s: _make_step_result(s, StepStatus.PASS),\n            check_gate=lambda r: _make_gate_result(\"task-2_remediation\", True),\n        )\n\n        assert retry_result.status == RemediationRetryStatus.PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT\n        assert retry_result.attempts_made == 1\n\n        # Conflict review (no intervening tasks in this scenario)\n        conflict = review_conflicts(\n            remediation_files={remediation_step.output_file},\n            intervening_files=set(),\n        )\n        assert conflict.action == ConflictAction.PASSTHROUGH\n\n        # Budget accounting\n        assert ledger.consumed == 15  # 10 original + 5 remediation\n        assert ledger.can_launch()  # Can continue\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Scenario 3: Task fails gate → remediation fails → HALT with diagnostic\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def test_scenario_3_fail_remediate_fail_halt(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Task fails gate, remediation persistently fails, HALT with diagnostic.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_remediation_budget=5)\n\n        gate = GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\"],\n            min_lines=10,\n            enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n        )\n        step = _make_step(\"task-3\", tmp_path, gate=gate)\n\n        # Task execution\n        ledger.debit(10)\n\n        # Gate fails\n        gate_result = _make_gate_result(\n            \"task-3\", passed=False,\n            reason=\"Output too short and missing frontmatter\",\n        )\n\n        # Build remediation\n        remediation_step = _make_step(\"task-3_remediation\", tmp_path)\n\n        # Both remediation attempts fail\n        retry_result = attempt_remediation(\n            remediation_step=remediation_step,\n            turns_per_attempt=5,\n            can_remediate=ledger.can_remediate,\n            debit=ledger.debit,\n            run_step=lambda s: _make_step_result(s, StepStatus.FAIL),\n            check_gate=lambda r: _make_gate_result(\n                \"task-3_remediation\", False, \"still broken\"\n            ),\n        )\n\n        assert retry_result.status == RemediationRetryStatus.PERSISTENT_FAILURE\n        assert retry_result.attempts_made == 2\n\n        # Both attempts' turns consumed\n        assert ledger.consumed == 20  # 10 original + 5 + 5 remediation\n\n        # Diagnostic chain fires\n        diagnostic = run_diagnostic_chain(\n            step_id=\"task-3\",\n            failure_reason=\"Output too short and missing frontmatter\",\n            remediation_output=\"\",\n        )\n        assert diagnostic.is_complete\n        assert diagnostic.stages_completed == 4\n\n        # Resume output generated\n        remaining = [\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T07.04\", title=\"Conflict review\"),\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T07.05\", title=\"Diagnostic chain\"),\n        ]\n        config = SprintConfig(index_path=Path(\"sprint.md\"), work_dir=tmp_path)\n        resume = build_resume_output(\n            config=config,\n            halt_task_id=\"T07.04\",\n            remaining_tasks=remaining,\n            diagnostic_path=str(tmp_path / \"diagnostic.md\"),\n            ledger=ledger,\n        )\n        assert \"--resume T07.04\" in resume\n        assert \"2\" in resume  # remaining count\n        assert \"diagnostic.md\" in resume\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Scenario 4: Low budget → skip remediation → HALT\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def test_scenario_4_low_budget_halt(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Low budget prevents remediation; HALT with budget message.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(\n            initial_budget=15,\n            minimum_remediation_budget=10,\n            minimum_allocation=5,\n        )\n\n        step = _make_step(\"task-4\", tmp_path)\n\n        # Task consumes most of the budget\n        ledger.debit(12)\n        assert ledger.available() == 3\n\n        # Gate fails\n        gate_result = _make_gate_result(\n            \"task-4\", passed=False,\n            reason=\"Gate failure\",\n        )\n\n        # Can't remediate (budget too low)\n        assert not ledger.can_remediate()\n\n        # Attempt remediation returns BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\n        remediation_step = _make_step(\"task-4_remediation\", tmp_path)\n        retry_result = attempt_remediation(\n            remediation_step=remediation_step,\n            turns_per_attempt=10,\n            can_remediate=ledger.can_remediate,\n            debit=ledger.debit,\n            run_step=lambda s: _make_step_result(s, StepStatus.PASS),\n            check_gate=lambda r: _make_gate_result(\"task-4_remediation\", True),\n        )\n\n        assert retry_result.status == RemediationRetryStatus.BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\n        assert retry_result.attempts_made == 0\n        assert retry_result.turns_consumed == 0\n\n        # Budget unchanged (no remediation was attempted)\n        assert ledger.consumed == 12\n\n        # No diagnostic chain for budget-specific halt (Gap 2, R-011)\n        # Resume output includes budget info\n        config = SprintConfig(index_path=Path(\"sprint.md\"), work_dir=tmp_path)\n        remaining = [TaskEntry(task_id=\"T07.05\", title=\"Next task\")]\n        resume = build_resume_output(\n            config=config,\n            halt_task_id=\"T07.05\",\n            remaining_tasks=remaining,\n            ledger=ledger,\n        )\n        assert \"--resume T07.05\" in resume\n        assert \"3\" in resume  # available turns\n        assert \"12\" in resume  # consumed turns\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Cross-scenario: Budget accounting consistency\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def test_budget_accounting_across_scenarios(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Verify TurnLedger state is consistent across multiple operations.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200, minimum_remediation_budget=5)\n\n        # Task 1: passes (10 consumed, 5 reimbursed)\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        ledger.credit(int(10 * ledger.reimbursement_rate))\n\n        # Task 2: fails, remediation passes (10 + 5 consumed)\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        retry = attempt_remediation(\n            remediation_step=_make_step(\"s2_rem\", tmp_path),\n            turns_per_attempt=5,\n            can_remediate=ledger.can_remediate,\n            debit=ledger.debit,\n            run_step=lambda s: _make_step_result(s, StepStatus.PASS),\n            check_gate=lambda r: _make_gate_result(\"s2\", True),\n        )\n        assert retry.status == RemediationRetryStatus.PASS_FIRST_ATTEMPT\n\n        # Verify cumulative accounting\n        # consumed: 10 + 10 + 5 = 25\n        # reimbursed: 5\n        # available: 200 - 25 + 5 = 180\n        assert ledger.consumed == 25\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == 5\n        assert ledger.available() == 180\n","numLines":330,"startLine":1,"totalLines":330}}}

  29→def _generate_100kb_output(path: Path) -> None:\n    30→    \"\"\"Generate a synthetic ~100KB NDJSON-like output file with valid frontmatter.\"\"\"\n    31→    lines = [\"---\", \"title:    f\"Timings: {[f'{t:.1f}' for t in timings]}\"\n   101→        )\n   102→\n   103→    @pytest.mark.gate_performance\n   104→    def test_gate_performance_evaluation_ms_populated(self, tmp_path):\n        return None if self._poll <= 1 else 0\n\n            def wait(self, timeout=None):\n                self.returncode = 0\n                return 0\n\n        def _factory(*args, **kwargs):\n **kwargs):\n            phase = config.phases[0]\n            config.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            config.output_file(phase).write_text(\"running\\n\")\n \"\"\"Tests for per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_tasks(count: int = 3) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n        return [\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=f\"T02.{i:02d}\",\n                title=f\"Task {i}\",\n                dependencies=[f\"T02.{i-1:02d}\"] if i > 1 else [],\n            )\n            for i in range(1, count + 1)\n        ]\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always succeeds with 3 turns consumed.\"\"\"\n        return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _fail_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always fails.\"\"\"\n        return (1, 5, 512)\n\n    def test_per_task_spawns_one_subprocess_per_task(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        spawn_count = [0]\n\n        def counting_factory(task, config, phase):\n            spawn_count[0] += 1\n            return (0, 2, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, _subprocess_factory=counting_factory\n        )\n        assert spawn_count[0] == 3\n        assert len(results) == 3\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def test_per_task_all_pass(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(2)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, _subprocess_factory=self._pass_factory\n        )\n        assert all(r.status == TaskStatus.PASS for r in results)\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def test_per_task_budget_prevents_starvation(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"If budget insufficient for next task, HALT with remaining task IDs.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        # Budget allows only 1 task launch (min_allocation=5, budget=8)\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=8, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def consume_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 5, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=consume_factory,\n        )\n        # First task succeeds, next two should be skipped due to budget\n        passed = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS]\n        skipped = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED]\n        assert len(passed) == 1\n        assert len(skipped) == 2\n        assert remaining == [\"T02.02\", \"T02.03\"]\n\n    def test_per_task_budget_debit_credit(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"TurnLedger debit/credit occurs per-task.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(1)\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50, minimum_allocation=5)\n        initial = ledger.available()\n\n        results, _ = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=self._pass_factory,\n        )\n        # Factory returns 3 turns consumed; ledger should reflect that\n        assert ledger.available() == initial - 3\n\n    def test_per_task_empty_inventory(self, tmp_path):\n        config =    )\n        assert results == []\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def test_per_task_fail_records_status(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = [\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T02.01\", title=\"Task 1\"),\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T02.02\", def test_can_remediate_allows_when_sufficient(self):\n        ledger = TurnLedger(\n            initial_budget=50,\n            minimum_allocation=5,\n            minimum_remediation_budget=3,\n        )\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is True\n\n    def test_turn_count_zero_reimburses_minimum(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"If a task uses 0 turns, minimum_allocation is credited back.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = [TaskEntry(task_id=\"T02.01\", title=\"Task 1\")]\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def zero_turn_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 0, 100)  # 0 turns consumed\n\n        execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=zero_turn_factory,\n        )\n        # Pre-debited 5, actual 0, so credited 5 back\n        # Net effect: 0 consumed\n        assert ledger.available() == 50\n\n\nclass TestIntegrationSubprocess:\n    \"\"\"Integration test: full per-task subprocess loop with budget tracking.\n\n    Exercises ≥5 tasks through parse → allocate → launch → collect → aggregate\n    with budget verification. (D-0012)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_tasks(count: int = 5) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n        return [\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=f\"T03.{i:02d}\",\n                title=f\"Integration Task {i}\",\n                description=f\"Test task {i} for integration\",\n                dependencies=[f\"T03.{i-1:02d}\"] if i > 1 else [],\n            )\n            for i in range(1, count + 1)\n        ]\n\n    def test_integration_subprocess_five_tasks_mixed_outcomes(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Full loop: 5 tasks with varied outcomes and budget accounting.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(5)\n\n        # Track per-task consumption: task 1=3, task 2=7, task 3=2, task 4=5, task 5=4\n        outcomes = [\n            (0, 3, 1024),    # PASS, 3 turns\n            (0, 7, 2048),    # PASS, 7 turns\n            (1, 2, 512),     # FAIL, 2 turns\n            (0, 5, 1536),    # PASS, 5 turns\n            (124, 4, 256),   # INCOMPLETE (timeout), 4 turns\n        ]\n        call_index = [0]\n\n        def mixed_factory(task, config, phase):\n            idx = call_index[0]\n            call_index[0] += 1\n            return outcomes[idx]\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_allocation=5)\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=mixed_factory,\n        )\n\n        # All 5 tasks launched (no budget exhaustion)\n        assert len(results) == 5\n        assert remaining == []\n        assert call_index[0] == 5\n\n        # Verify per-task statuses\n        assert results[0].status == TaskStatus.PASS\n        assert results[1].status == TaskStatus.PASS\n        assert results[2].status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n        assert results[3].status == TaskStatus.PASS\n        assert results[4].status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n\n        # Budget accounting: available reflects actual turn consumption\n        # The executor pre-debits minimum_allocation (5) per task, then reconciles.\n        # Net effect on available: initial - sum(actual_turns)\n        actual_turns = [3, 7, 2, 5, 4]\n        net_consumed = sum(actual_turns)  # 21\n        assert ledger.available() == 100 - net_consumed  # = 79\n        # Verify internal accounting is consistent\n        assert ledger.available() == ledger.initial_budget - ledger.consumed + ledger.reimbursed\n\n        # Aggregate into phase report\n        report = aggregate_task_results(\n            phase_number=1, task_results=results, remaining_task_ids=remaining\n        )\n        assert report.tasks_total == 5\n        assert report.tasks_passed == 3\n        assert report.tasks_failed == 1\n        assert report.tasks_incomplete == 1\n        assert report.status == \"PARTIAL\"\n        assert report.total_turns_consumed == sum(r.turns_consumed for r in results)\n\n    def test_integration_subprocess_budget_exhaustion_skips_remaining(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Budget exhaustion after 3 tasks skips remaining 2.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(5)\n\n        # Each task consumes 8 turns; budget=25, minimum_allocation=5\n        # Task 1: debit 5 upfront, then adjust to 8 → consumed=8, avail=17\n        # Task 2: debit 5 upfront, then adjust to 8 → consumed=16, avail=9\n        # Task 3: debit 5 upfront, then adjust to 8 → consumed=24, avail=1\n        # Task 4: can_launch? avail=1 < min_alloc=5 → SKIP\n        def heavy_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 8, 500)\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=25, minimum_allocation=5)\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=heavy_factory,\n        )\n\n        passed = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS]\n        skipped = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED]\n        assert len(passed) == 3\n        assert len(skipped) == 2\n        assert remaining == [\"T03.04\", \"T03.05\"]\n\n        # Aggregate reflects skipped tasks\n        report = aggregate_task_results(1, results, remaining)\n        assert report.tasks_total == 7  # 5 results + 2 remaining\n        assert report.tasks_skipped == 2\n\n    def test_integration_subprocess_all_pass_aggregation(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"All 5 tasks pass → PASS status and CONTINUE recommendation.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(5)\n\n        def pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_allocation=5)\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=pass_factory,\n        )\n\n        assert all(r.status == TaskStatus.PASS for r in results)\n        assert remaining == []\n\n        report = aggregate_task_results(1, results, remaining)\n        assert report.status == \"PASS\"\n        md = report.to_markdown()\n        assert \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in md\n        assert \"tasks_total: 5\" in md\n        assert \"tasks_passed: 5\" in md\n\n        # Budget: 5 tasks * 3 turns each = 15 net consumed\n        # available = initial - net consumed = 100 - 15 = 85\n        assert ledger.available() == 85\n\n\nclass TestBackwardCompat:\n    \"\"\"Backward compatibility tests: grace_period=0 produces v1.2.1 behavior.\n\n    Verifies that the default configuration (grace_period=0, GateMode.BLOCKING)\n    produces results identical to pre-trailing-gate behavior with zero\n    additional daemon threads from the gate system. confirm default\n\n        class _PassPopen:\n            def __init__(self):\n                self.returncode = 0\n                self.pid = 2001\n                self._poll = 0\n\n            def poll(self):\n                self._poll += 1\n                return None if self._poll <= 1 else 0\n\n            def wait(self, timeout=None):\n                self.returncode = 0\n                return 0\n\n        def _factory(*args, **kwargs):\n            phase = config.phases[0]\n            config.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            config.result_file(phase).write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n            config.output_file(phase).write_text(\"output\\n\")\n            return _PassPopen()\n\n        baseline_threads = threading.active_count()\n\n        captured = []\n        with (\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.executor.shutil.which\", return_value=\"/usr/bin/claude\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.pipeline.process.subprocess.Popen\", side_effect=_factory),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.pipeline.process.os.setpgrp\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.notify._notify\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.executor.SprintLogger\") as logger_cls,\n        ):\n            logger = MagicMock()\n            logger.write_summary = MagicMock(side_effect=lambda sr: captured.append(sr))\n            logger_cls.return_value = logger\n            execute_sprint(config)\n\n        # {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011QchyvxLfHAYNVv8ZNKnz6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"Pipeline executor -- generic step sequencer with retry, gates, and parallel dispatch.\n     2→\n     3→Composition-via-callable design: consumers (sprint, roadmap) inject their own\n     4→StepRunner and callbacks. The executor handles step ordering, retry logic,\n     5→parallel dispatch, and state management.\n     6→\n     7→NFR-007: No imports from superclaude.cli.sprint or superclaude.cli.roadmap.\n     8→\"\"\"\n     9→\n    10→from __future__ import annotations\n    11→\n    12→import logging\n    13→import threading\n    14→from datetime import datetime, timezone\n    15→from typing import Callable, Protocol\n    16→\n    17→from .gates import gate_passed\n    18→from .models import GateMode, PipelineConfig, Step, StepResult, StepStatus\n    19→from .trailing_gate import TrailingGateRunner\n    20→\n    21→_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.pipeline.executor\")\n    22→\n    23→\n    24→class StepRunner(Protocol):\n    25→    \"\"\"Callable that executes a single step's subprocess and returns a result.\n    26→\n    27→    The runner is responsible for:\n    28→    - Launching the claude -p subprocess\n    29→    - Waiting for completion (with timeout enforcement)\n    30→    - Returning a StepResult with appropriate status\n    31→\n    32→    The runner is NOT responsible for:\n    33→    - Retry logic (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    34→    - Gate checking (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    35→    - Step ordering (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    36→    \"\"\"\n    37→\n    38→    def __call__(\n    39→        self,\n    40→        step: Step,\n    41→        config: PipelineConfig,\n    42→        cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n    43→    ) -> StepResult: ...\n    44→\n    45→\n    46→def execute_pipeline(\n    47→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n    48→    config: PipelineConfig,\n    49→    run_step: StepRunner,\n    50→    on_step_start: Callable[[Step], None] = lambda s: None,\n    51→    on_step_complete: Callable[[Step, StepResult], None] = lambda s, r: None,\n    52→    on_state_update: Callable[[dict], None] = lambda state: None,\n    53→    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool] = lambda: False,\n    54→    trailing_runner: TrailingGateRunner | None = None,\n    55→) -> list[StepResult]:\n    56→    \"\"\"Generic pipeline executor.\n    57→\n    58→    Processes steps in order. Each element in ``steps`` is either:\n    59→    - A single Step (executed sequentially)\n    60→    - A list[Step] (all steps in the list executed in parallel)\n    61→\n    62→    For each step:\n    63→    1. Call on_step_start(step)\n    64→    2. Call run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\n    65→    3. If step has gate criteria: run gate_passed()\n    66→       - BLOCKING mode: evaluate synchronously, halt on failure\n    67→       - TRAILING mode (grace_period > 0): submit to TrailingGateRunner,\n    68→         continue execution; sync point at end collects results\n    69→    4. Returns when all complete or one fails.\n   260→\n   261→    Cross-cancellation: if any step fails after retry, a shared Event\n   262→    is set, causing remaining steps to terminate their subprocesses.\n   263→    \"\"\"\n   264→    cancel_event = threading.Event()\n   265→    results: list[StepResult | None] = [None] * len(steps)\n   266→\n   267→    def _worker(idx: int, step: Step) -> None:\n   268→        def combined_cancel() -> bool:\n   269→            return cancel_check() or cancel_event.is_set()\n   270→\n   271→        result = _execute_single_step(step, config, run_step, combined_cancel)\n   272→        results[idx] = result\n   273→        if result.status != StepStatus.PASS:\n   274→            cancel_event.set()\n   275→\n   276→    threads = [\n   277→        threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(i, s), daemon=True)\n   278→        for i, s in enumerate(steps)\n   279→    ]\n   280→    for t in threads:\n   281→        t.start()\n   282→    for t in threads:\n   283→        t.join()\n   284→\n   285→    # Replace None entries (shouldn't happen, but defensive)\n   286→    final: list[StepResult] = []\n   287→    for i, r in enumerate(results):\n   288→        if r is not None:\n   289→            final.append(r)\n   290→        else:\n   291→            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   292→            final.append(StepResult(\n   293→                step=steps[i],\n   294→                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n   295→                attempt=0,\n   296→                gate_failure_reason=\"Thread did not produce a result\",\n   297→                started_at=now,\n   298→                finished_at=now,\n   299→            ))\n   300→\n   301→    return final\n   302→\n   303→\n   304→def _build_state(results: list[StepResult]) -> dict:\n   305→    \"\"\"Build a state dict from accumulated results.\"\"\"\n   306→    return {\n   307→        \"steps\": {\n   308→            r.step.id: {\n   309→                \"status\": r.status.value,\n   310→                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n   311→                \"gate_failure_reason\": r.gate_failure_reason,\n   312→            }\n   313→            for r in results\n   314→        },\n   315→        \"total\": len(results),\n   316→        \"passed\": sum(1 for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS),\n   317→        \"failed\": sum(1 for r in results if r.status.is_failure),\n   318→    }\n   319→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9a0d9800-2da9-4f6b-b8d7-49e79acf4bb2","uuid":"559c2123-a90f-40ba-9772-d06294a68cea","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/executor.py","content":"\"\"\"Pipeline executor -- generic step sequencer with retry, gates, and parallel dispatch.\n\nComposition-via-callable design: consumers (sprint, roadmap) inject their own\nStepRunner and callbacks. The executor handles step ordering, retry logic,\nparallel dispatch, and state management.\n\nNFR-007: No imports from superclaude.cli.sprint or superclaude.cli.roadmap.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport threading\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom typing import Callable, Protocol\n\nfrom .gates import gate_passed\nfrom .models import GateMode, PipelineConfig, Step, StepResult, StepStatus\nfrom .trailing_gate import TrailingGateRunner\n\n_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.pipeline.executor\")\n\n\nclass StepRunner(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Callable that executes a single step's subprocess and returns a result.\n\n    The runner is responsible for:\n    - Launching the claude -p subprocess\n    - Waiting for completion (with timeout enforcement)\n    - Returning a StepResult with appropriate status\n\n    The runner is NOT responsible for:\n    - Retry logic (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    - Gate checking (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    - Step ordering (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __call__(\n        self,\n        step: Step,\n        config: PipelineConfig,\n        cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n    ) -> StepResult: ...\n\n\ndef execute_pipeline(\n    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    run_step: StepRunner,\n    on_step_start: Callable[[Step], None] = lambda s: None,\n    on_step_complete: Callable[[Step, StepResult], None] = lambda s, r: None,\n    on_state_update: Callable[[dict], None] = lambda state: None,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool] = lambda: False,\n    trailing_runner: TrailingGateRunner | None = None,\n) -> list[StepResult]:\n    \"\"\"Generic pipeline executor.\n\n    Processes steps in order. Each element in ``steps`` is either:\n    - A single Step (executed sequentially)\n    - A list[Step] (all steps in the list executed in parallel)\n\n    For each step:\n    1. Call on_step_start(step)\n    2. Call run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\n    3. If step has gate criteria: run gate_passed()\n       - BLOCKING mode: evaluate synchronously, halt on failure\n       - TRAILING mode (grace_period > 0): submit to TrailingGateRunner,\n         continue execution; sync point at end collects results\n    4. If gate fails and attempts < retry_limit: retry (go to 2)\n    5. If gate fails and attempts exhausted: HALT\n    6. Call on_step_complete(step, result)\n    7. Call on_state_update(updated_state_dict)\n\n    For parallel step groups:\n    - All steps in the group run concurrently via _run_parallel_steps()\n    - If any step in the group fails, remaining steps are cancelled\n    - All results from the group must be PASS before proceeding\n\n    Returns list of all StepResults (one per step, flattened).\n    \"\"\"\n    all_results: list[StepResult] = []\n    # Create trailing runner if grace_period > 0 and none provided\n    _trailing = trailing_runner\n    if _trailing is None and config.grace_period > 0:\n        _trailing = TrailingGateRunner()\n\n    for entry in steps:\n        if cancel_check():\n            break\n\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            # Parallel step group\n            for s in entry:\n                on_step_start(s)\n\n            group_results = _run_parallel_steps(entry, config, run_step, cancel_check)\n            all_results.extend(group_results)\n\n            for r in group_results:\n                on_step_complete(r.step, r)\n\n            on_state_update(_build_state(all_results))\n\n            # If any step in the group failed, halt\n            if any(r.status != StepStatus.PASS for r in group_results):\n                break\n        else:\n            # Sequential step -- branch on gate_mode\n            result = _execute_single_step(\n                entry, config, run_step, cancel_check,\n                on_step_start, on_step_complete,\n                trailing_runner=_trailing,\n            )\n            all_results.append(result)\n            on_state_update(_build_state(all_results))\n\n            if result.status != StepStatus.PASS:\n                break\n\n    # Sync point: collect all trailing gate results at end of pipeline\n    if _trailing is not None:\n        trailing_results = _trailing.wait_for_pending(\n            timeout=max(30.0, float(config.grace_period))\n        )\n        for tr in trailing_results:\n            if not tr.passed:\n                _log.warning(\n                    \"Trailing gate failed for step '%s': %s\",\n                    tr.step_id,\n                    tr.failure_reason,\n                )\n\n    return all_results\n\n\ndef _execute_single_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    run_step: StepRunner,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n    on_step_start: Callable[[Step], None] = lambda s: None,\n    on_step_complete: Callable[[Step, StepResult], None] = lambda s, r: None,\n    trailing_runner: TrailingGateRunner | None = None,\n) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single step with retry logic and gate checking.\n\n    Gate mode branching:\n    - BLOCKING (default): gate evaluates synchronously before proceeding\n    - TRAILING (grace_period > 0): gate submitted to TrailingGateRunner,\n      step returns PASS immediately and execution continues\n    - grace_period == 0 forces BLOCKING regardless of gate_mode\n    \"\"\"\n    on_step_start(step)\n    max_attempts = step.retry_limit + 1  # retry_limit=1 means 2 attempts total\n\n    # Determine effective gate mode\n    effective_mode = step.gate_mode\n    if config.grace_period == 0:\n        effective_mode = GateMode.BLOCKING\n\n    for attempt in range(1, max_attempts + 1):\n        if cancel_check():\n            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            result = StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=attempt,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n                started_at=now,\n                finished_at=now,\n            )\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        result = run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\n        result = StepResult(\n            step=result.step,\n            status=result.status,\n            attempt=attempt,\n            gate_failure_reason=result.gate_failure_reason,\n            started_at=result.started_at,\n            finished_at=result.finished_at,\n        )\n\n        # If step has no gate, trust the StepRunner's status\n        if step.gate is None:\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        # If runner already reported failure/timeout, skip gate check\n        if result.status in (StepStatus.TIMEOUT, StepStatus.CANCELLED):\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        # TRAILING mode: submit to runner, return PASS immediately\n        if effective_mode == GateMode.TRAILING and trailing_runner is not None:\n            trailing_runner.submit(step)\n            result = StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.PASS,\n                attempt=attempt,\n                gate_failure_reason=None,\n                started_at=result.started_at,\n                finished_at=result.finished_at,\n            )\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        # BLOCKING mode: run gate check synchronously\n        passed, reason = gate_passed(step.output_file, step.gate)\n        if passed:\n            result = StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.PASS,\n                attempt=attempt,\n                gate_failure_reason=None,\n                started_at=result.started_at,\n                finished_at=result.finished_at,\n            )\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        # Gate failed\n        _log.info(\"Gate failed for step '%s' (attempt %d/%d): %s\", step.id, attempt, max_attempts, reason)\n\n        if attempt < max_attempts:\n            continue  # retry\n\n        # Exhausted retries\n        result = StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=attempt,\n            gate_failure_reason=reason,\n            started_at=result.started_at,\n            finished_at=result.finished_at,\n        )\n        on_step_complete(step, result)\n        return result\n\n    # Should not reach here, but safety fallback\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n        attempt=max_attempts,\n        gate_failure_reason=\"Unexpected: exhausted retry loop\",\n        started_at=now,\n        finished_at=now,\n    )\n\n\ndef _run_parallel_steps(\n    steps: list[Step],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    run_step: StepRunner,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> list[StepResult]:\n    \"\"\"Run steps concurrently. Returns when all complete or one fails.\n\n    Cross-cancellation: if any step fails after retry, a shared Event\n    is set, causing remaining steps to terminate their subprocesses.\n    \"\"\"\n    cancel_event = threading.Event()\n    results: list[StepResult | None] = [None] * len(steps)\n\n    def _worker(idx: int, step: Step) -> None:\n        def combined_cancel() -> bool:\n            return cancel_check() or cancel_event.is_set()\n\n        result = _execute_single_step(step, config, run_step, combined_cancel)\n        results[idx] = result\n        if result.status != StepStatus.PASS:\n            cancel_event.set()\n\n    threads = [\n        threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(i, s), daemon=True)\n        for i, s in enumerate(steps)\n    ]\n    for t in threads:\n        t.start()\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    # Replace None entries (shouldn't happen, but defensive)\n    final: list[StepResult] = []\n    for i, r in enumerate(results):\n        if r is not None:\n            final.append(r)\n        else:\n            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            final.append(StepResult(\n                step=steps[i],\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=0,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Thread did not produce a result\",\n                started_at=now,\n                finished_at=now,\n            ))\n\n    return final\n\n\ndef _build_state(results: list[StepResult]) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Build a state dict from accumulated results.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"steps\": {\n            r.step.id: {\n                \"status\": r.status.value,\n                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n                \"gate_failure_reason\": r.gate_failure_reason,\n            }\n            for r in results\n        },\n        \"total\": len(results),\n        \"passed\": sum(1 for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS),\n        \"failed\": sum(1 for r in results if r.status.is_failure),\n    }\n","numLines":319,"startLine":1,"totalLines":319}}}

_make_step(\"T09.01\", tmp_path, gate=GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\"],\n            min_lines=5,\n            enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n        ))\n        ledger.debit(10)\n\n        # Step 2: Trailing gate evaluation (fails)\n        gate_result = _make_gate_result(\n            \"T09.01\", passed=False,\n            reason=\"Missing required field 'title'\",\n        )\n\n        # Record in deferred remediation log\n        remediation_log = DeferredRemediationLog(persist_path=tmp_path / \"remediation.json\")\n (verifies context injection)\n        prompt = build_remediation_prompt(gate_result, step, file_paths={step.output_file})\n        assert \"Missing required field 'title'\" in prompt\n        assert            remediation_step=remediation_step,\n            turns_per_attempt=5,\n            can_remediate=ledger.can_remediate,\n            debit=ledger.debit,\n            run_step=lambda s: # Step 5: Context injection from prior task results\n        task_entry = TaskEntry(task_id=\"T09.01\", title=\"E2E task 1\")\n        task_result = TaskResult(\n            task=task_entry,\n            status=TaskStatus.PASS,\n            turns_consumed=10,\n            gate_outcome=GateOutcome.PASS,\n        )\n        context = task_result.to_context_summary(verbose=True)\n        assert budget_remaining=ledger.available(),\n        )\n        assert report.status == \"PASS\"\n        assert report.budget_remaining == ledger.available()\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Helpers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _make_config(tmp_path: Path, grace_period: int = 10) -> SprintConfig:\n    phases = []\n    for i in range(1, 3):\n        pf = tmp_path / f\"phase-{i}-tasklist.md\"\n        pf.write_text(f\"# Phase {i}\\n\")\n        phases.append(Phase(number=i, file=pf, name=f\"Phase {i}\"))\n\n    index = tmp_path / \"tasklist-index.md\"\n    index.write_text(\"index\\n\")\n    return SprintConfig(\n        index_path=index,\n        release_dir=tmp_path,\n        phases=phases,\n        start_phase=1,\n        end_phase=2,\n        max_turns=50,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_tasks(count: int = 5) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n    \"\"\"Create a multi-task inventory for E2E testing.\"\"\"\n    return [\n        TaskEntry(task_id=f\"T09.{i:02d}\", title=f\"E2E task {i}\")\n        for i in range(1, count + 1)\n    ]\n\n\ndef _make_step(step_id: str, tmp_path: Path, gate: GateCriteria | None = None) -> Step:\n    return Step(\n        id=step_id,\n        prompt=f\"Execute {step_id}\",\n        output_file=tmp_path / f\"{step_id}_output.md\",\n        gate=gate,\n        timeout_seconds=60,\n        gate_mode=GateMode.TRAILING,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_gate_result(step_id: str, passed: bool, reason: str | None = None) -> TrailingGateResult:\n    return TrailingGateResult(\n        step_id=step_id, passed=passed,\n        evaluation_ms=5.0, failure_reason=reason,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_step_result(step: Step, status: StepStatus = StepStatus.PASS) -> StepResult:\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    return StepResult(step=step, status=status, started_at=now, finished_at=now)\n\n\ndef _write_passing_output(path: Path, lines: int = 20) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write a file that passes STANDARD gate checks.\"\"\"\n    content = [\"---\", \"title: test output\", \"status: pass\", \"---\"]\n    content.extend([f\"Line {i}: content\" for i in range(lines)])\n    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(content))\n\n\n# ===========================================================================\n# T09.01 — End-to-End Sprint Test with Trailing Gates\n# ===========================================================================\n\n\nclass TestE2ETrailingGates:\n    \"\"\"End-to-end acceptance test: multi-task sprint with trailing gate mode.\n\n    Exercises the complete flow: budget → subprocess → gate → remediation\n    → context → report.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Core E2E: multi-task sprint with budget and trailing gates\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @pytest.mark.e2e_trailing\n    def test_multi_task_sprint_with_trailing_gates(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Multi-task sprint completes with correct per-task results under\n        trailing gate mode: all tasks pass, gates evaluate asynchronously,\n        budget accounting is correct, and phase report is generated.\"\"\"\n        # Setup\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200, minimum_allocation=5)\n        tasks = _make_tasks(4)\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, grace_period=10)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n\n        # Mock subprocess: all tasks succeed, consume varying turns\n        turn_costs = [8, 12, 6, 10]  # per-task turn costs\n\n        def subprocess_factory(task, cfg, ph):\n            idx = int(task.task_id.split(\".\")[-1]) - 1\n            return (0, turn_costs[idx], 1024 * (idx + 1))\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase,\n            ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=subprocess_factory,\n        )\n\n        # All 4 tasks should have results, none remaining\n        assert len(results) == 4\n        assert len(remaining) == 0\n\n        # All tasks passed\n        assert all(r.status == TaskStatus.PASS for r in results)\n\n        # Budget accounting identity: available == initial - consumed + reimbursed\n        assert ledger.available() == ledger.initial_budget - ledger.consumed + ledger.reimbursed\n\n        # Consumed should reflect actual turn costs with pre-allocation reconciliation\n        # Pre-alloc is 5 per task; actual costs are [8, 12, 6, 10]\n        # Task 1: debit(5), actual=8, additional debit(3) → total consumed so far: 8\n        # Task 2: debit(5), actual=12, additional debit(7) → total consumed: 20\n        # Task 3: debit(5), actual=6, additional debit(1) → total consumed: 26\n        # Task 4: debit(5), actual=10, additional debit(5) → total consumed: 36\n        assert ledger.consumed == sum(turn_costs)\n\n        # Now verify trailing gate evaluation\n        gate = GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\"],\n            min_lines=3,\n            enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n        )\n        runner = TrailingGateRunner()\n\n        for i, result in enumerate(results):\n            step = _make_step(result.task.task_id, tmp_path, gate=gate)\n            _write_passing_output(step.output_file)\n            runner.submit(step)\n\n        # Wait for all trailing gates\n        gate_results = runner.wait_for_pending(timeout=10.0)\n        gate_results.extend(runner.drain())\n        assert len(gate_results) == 4\n        assert all(gr.passed for gr in gate_results)\n\n        # Generate phase report\n        report = aggregate_task_results(\n            phase_number=1,\n            task_results=results,\n            budget_remaining=ledger.available(),\n        )\n\n        assert report.status == \"PASS\"\n        assert report.tasks_total == 4\n        assert report.tasks_passed == 4\n        assert report.tasks_failed == 0\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Budget accounting identity\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @pytest.mark.e2e_trailing\n    def test_budget_accounting_identity(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Budget accounting identity: total consumed + remaining == initial budget.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_allocation=5)\n        tasks = _make_tasks(3)\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n\n        def subprocess_factory(task, cfg, ph):\n            return (0, 10, 500)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase,\n            ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=subprocess_factory,\n        )\n\n        # Accounting identity: consumed - reimbursed + available == initial_budget\n        assert ledger.consumed - ledger.reimbursed + ledger.available() == ledger.initial_budget\n        # Equivalent: available == initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed\n    @pytest.mark.e2e_trailing\n    def test_gate_mode_resolution_with_grace_period(self):\n        \"\"\"Trailing gate mode activates when grace_period > 0 for task scope.\"\"\"\n        # Task scope: trailing when grace_period > 0\n        mode = resolve_gate_mode(GateScope.TASK, grace_period=10)\n        assert mode == GateMode.TRAILING\n\n        # Task scope: blocking when grace_period == 0\n        mode = resolve_gate_mode(GateScope.TASK, grace_period=0)\n        assert mode == GateMode.BLOCKING\n\n        # Release scope: always blocking (immutable)\n        mode = resolve_gate_mode(GateScope.RELEASE, grace_period=999)\n        assert mode == GateMode.BLOCKING\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Mixed outcomes: pass, fail, incomplete in one sprint\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @pytest.mark.e2e_trailing\n    def test_mixed_outcomes_comprehensive(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Multi-task sprint with mixed outcomes: PASS, FAIL, INCOMPLETE.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200, minimum_allocation=5)\n        tasks = _make_tasks(5)\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n\n        # exit codes: 0=PASS, 1=FAIL, 0=PASS, 124=INCOMPLETE, 0=PASS\n        outcomes = [(0, 10, 500), (1, 8, 300), (0, 12, 600), (124, 5, 200), (0, 7, 400)]\n\n        def subprocess_factory(task, cfg, ph):\n            idx = int(task.task_id.split(\".\")[-1]) - 1\n            return outcomes[idx]\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase,\n            ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=subprocess_factory,\n        )\n\n        assert len(results) == 5\n        statuses = [r.status for r in results]\n        assert statuses == [\n            TaskStatus.PASS,\n            TaskStatus.FAIL,\n            TaskStatus.PASS,\n            TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE,\n            TaskStatus.PASS,\n        ]\n\n        # Phase report reflects mixed outcomes\n        report = aggregate_task_results(\n            phase_number=1,\n            task_results=results,\n            budget_remaining=ledger.available(),\n        )\n        assert report.tasks_passed == 3\n        assert report.tasks_failed == 1\n        assert report.tasks_incomplete == 1\n        assert report.status == \"PARTIAL\"\n\n        # Budget identity still holds\n        assert ledger.available() == ledger.initial_budget - ledger.consumed + ledger.reimbursed\n\n    # persistent failure: diagnostic chain fires, resume output\n        includes remaining tasks and budget info.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_remediation_budget=5)\n      config = _make_config(tmp_path)\n        resume = build_resume_output(\n            config=config,\n            halt_task_id=\"T09.04\",\n            remaining_tasks=remaining,\n No resume needed on success\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Phase status determination: v1.2.1 priority chain\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @pytest.mark.backward_compat\n    def test_phase_status_priority_chain_preserved(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"_determine_phase_status() priority chain is unchanged from v1.2.1:\n        timeout(124) > error(!=0) > HALT > CONTINUE > frontmatter > no_report.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.sprint.executor import _determine_phase_status\n\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        # 1. Timeout always wins\n        output_file.write_text(\"content\")\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(124, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n\n        # 2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n        assert _determine_phase_status(1, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n        # 3. HALT signal wins over CONTINUE when both present\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n        # 4. CONTINUE signal -> PASS\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n        # 5. Frontmatter status: PASS -> PASS\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: PASS\\n---\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n        # 6. Frontmatter status: FAIL -> HALT\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: FAIL\\n---\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n        # 7. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n        result_file.unlink()\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n        # 8. No result file, no output -> ERROR\n        output_file.unlink()\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # EXEMPT: always passes\n        gate = GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[], min_lines=0,\n            enforcement_tier=\"EXEMPT\",\n        )\n        passed, reason = gate_passed(output_file, gate)\n        assert passed is True\n\n        # No resume needed on success\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Phase status determination: v1.2.1 priority chain\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @pytest.mark.backward_compat\n    def test_phase_status_priority_chain_preserved(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"_determine_phase_status() priority chain is unchanged from v1.2.1:\n        timeout(124) > error(!=0) > HALT > CONTINUE > frontmatter > no_report.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.sprint.executor import _determine_phase_status\n\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        # 1. Timeout always wins\n        output_file.write_text(\"content\")\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(124, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n\n        # 2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n        assert _determine_phase_status(1, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n        # 3. HALT signal wins over CONTINUE when both present\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n        # 4. CONTINUE signal -> PASS\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n        # 5. Frontmatter status: PASS -> PASS\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: PASS\\n---\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n        # 6. Frontmatter status: FAIL -> HALT\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: FAIL\\n---\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n        # 7. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n        result_file.unlink()\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n        # 8. No result file, no output -> ERROR\n        output_file.unlink()\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # 1. Timeout always wins\n        output_file.write_text(\"content\")\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(124, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n\n        # 2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n        assert _determine_phase_status(1, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n        # 3. HALT signal wins over CONTINUE when both present\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n        # 4. CONTINUE signal -> PASS\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n        # 5. Frontmatter status: PASS -> PASS\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: PASS\\n---\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n        # 6. Frontmatter status: FAIL -> HALT\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: FAIL\\n---\\n\")\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n        # 7. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n        result_file.unlink()\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n        # 8. No result file, no output -> ERROR\n        output_file.unlink()\n        assert _determine_phase_status(0, result_file, output_file) == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # gate_passed: v1.2.1 tier behavior preserved\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @pytest.mark.backward_compat\n    def test_gate_passed_tier_behavior_preserved(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"gate_passed() tier-proportional checks are unchanged from v1.2.1.\"\"\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.md\"\n\n        # EXEMPT: always passes\n        gate = GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[], min_lines=0,\n            enforcement_tier=\"EXEMPT\",\n        )\n        passed, reason = gate_passed(output_file, gate)\n        assert passed is True\n\n        # LIGHT: file must exist and be non-empty\n        gate = GateCriteria(\n            required_frontmatter_fields=[], min_lines=0,\n            enforcement_tier=\"LIGHT\",\n        )\n        passed, reason = gate_passed(output_file, gate)\n        assert passed is False  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Multi-phase sprint: v1.2.1 execution log format\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @pytest.mark.backward_compat\n    def test_execution_log_format_v121(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Sprint execution log (JSONL) has v1.2.1-compatible event format.\"\"\"\n        import json\n\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n\n        def _factory(*args, **kwargs):\n            phase = config.phases[0]\n            config.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            config.result_file(phase).write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n            config.output_file(phase).write_text(\"output\\n\")\n            return _FakePopenPass()\n\n        with (\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.executor.shutil.which\", return_value=\"/usr/bin/claude\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.pipeline.process.subprocess.Popen\", side_effect=_factory),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.pipeline.process.os.setpgrp\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.notify._notify\"),\n        ):\n            execute_sprint(config)\n\n        jsonl_path = config.execution_log_jsonl\n        assert jsonl_path.exists()\n        events = [json.loads(line) for line in jsonl_path.read_text().strip().split(\"\\n\")]\n\n        # Budget monotonicity: TurnLedger.consumed never decreases\n2. Gate result ordering: GateResultQueue results arrive in submission order per step_id\n3. Remediation idempotency: double mark_remediated produces identical state\n\nAcceptance criteria (D-0040):\n- Budget monotonicity property holds across >=100 randomized operation sequences\n- Gate result ordering property holds across >=100 randomized concurrent submission scenarios\n- Remediation idempotency property holds: double mark_remediated produces identical state\n- `uv run pytest tests/ -k property_based` exits 0\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport threading\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport pytest\nfrom hypothesis import given, settings, assume\nfrom hypothesis import strategies as st\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.trailing_gate import (\n    DeferredRemediationLog,\n    GateResultQueue,\n    RemediationStatus,\n    TrailingGateResult,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.sprint.models import TurnLedger\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Strategies\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n# Non-negative integers for budget operations\nbudget_amount = st.integers(min_value=0, max_value=1000)\npositive_budget = st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=10000)\n\n\n# Debit/credit operation sequences\n@st.composite\ndef budget_operation_sequence(draw):\n    \"\"\"Generate a random sequence of (op_type, amount) tuples.\"\"\"\n    ops = draw(st.lists(\n        st.tuples(\n            st.sampled_from([\"debit\", \"credit\"]),\n            budget_amount,\n        ),\n        min_size=1,\n        max_size=200,\n    ))\n    return ops\n\n\n# Gate results with varying step_ids\n@st.composite\ndef gate_result_batch(draw):\n    \"\"\"Generate a batch of TrailingGateResult with sequential step_ids.\"\"\"\n    count = draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=50))\n    results = []\n    for i in range(count):\n        results.append(TrailingGateResult(\n            step_id=f\"step-{i}\",\n            passed=draw(st.booleans()),\n            evaluation_ms=draw(st.floats(min_value=0.0, max_value=1000.0)),\n            failure_reason=\"fail\" if not results or draw(st.booleans()) else None,\n        ))\n    return results\n\n\n# ===========================================================================\n# Property 1: TurnLedger Budget Monotonicity\n# sequence.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @given(initial=positive_budget, ops=budget_operation_sequence())\n    @settings(max_examples=200, deadline=None)\n    def test_consumed_never_decreases(self, initial, ops):\n        \"\"\"Property: for any sequence of debit/credit operations,\n        TurnLedger.consumed is monotonically non-decreasing.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=initial)\n        prev_consumed = 0\n\n        for op_type, amount in ops:\n            if op_type == \"debit\":\n                ledger.debit(amount)\n            else:\n                ledger.credit(amount)\n\n            # INVARIANT: consumed never decreases\n            assert ledger.consumed >= prev_consumed, (\n                f\"Monotonicity violated: consumed went from \"\n                f\"{prev_consumed} to {ledger.consumed} after {op_type}({amount})\"\n            )\n            prev_consumed = ledger.consumed\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @given(initial=positive_budget, ops=budget_operation_sequence())\n    @settings(max_examples=200, deadline=None)\n    def test_accounting_identity_holds(self, initial, ops):\n        \"\"\"Property: available() == initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed\n        after any sequence of operations.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=initial)\n\n        for op_type, amount in ops:\n            if op_type == \"debit\":\n                ledger.debit(amount)\n            else:\n                ledger.credit(amount)\n\n        # INVARIANT: accounting identity\n        assert ledger.available() == ledger.initial_budget - ledger.consumed + ledger.reimbursed\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @given(initial=positive_budget, debits=st.lists(budget_amount, min_size=1, max_size=100))\n    @settings(max_examples=200, deadline=None)\n    def test_consumed_equals_sum_of_debits(self, initial, debits):\n        \"\"\"Property: consumed == sum of all debit amounts.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=initial)\n\n        for amount in debits:\n            ledger.debit(amount)\n\n        assert ledger.consumed == for amount in credits:\n            ledger.credit(amount)\n\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == sum(credits)\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @given(amount=st.integers(min_value=-1000, max_value=-1))\n    @settings(max_examples=50, deadline=None)\n    def test_negative_debit_raises(self, amount):\n        \"\"\"Property: negative debit amounts always raise ValueError.\"\"\"\n queue.drain()\n        assert len(drained) == len(batch)\n\n        # All step_ids present\n        submitted_ids = [r.step_id for r in batch]\n        drained_ids = [r.step_id for r in drained]\n test_fifo_ordering_preserved(self, batch):\n        \"\"\"Property: results are drained in FIFO order (submission order).\"\"\"\n        queue = GateResultQueue()\n\n        for result in batch:\n            queue.put(result)\n\n        drained = queue.drain()\n\n        # FIFO: order preserved\n        for i, (submitted, received) in enumerate(zip(batch, drained)):\n            assert submitted.step_id == received.step_id, (\n                f\"Order violated at position {i}: expected {submitted.step_id}, \"\n                f\"got {received.step_id}\"\n            )\n            assert submitted.passed == received.passed\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @settings(max_examples=100, deadline=None)\n    @given(count=st.integers(min_value=2, max_value=20))\n    def test_concurrent_put_no_loss(self, count):\n        \"\"\"Property: concurrent put() from multiple threads loses no results.\"\"\"\n        queue = GateResultQueue()\n        barrier = threading.Barrier(count)\n\n        def _producer(idx):\n            barrier.wait()\n            queue.put(TrailingGateResult(\n                step_id=f\"step-{idx}\",\n                passed=True,\n                evaluation_ms=1.0,\n            ))\n\n        threads = [threading.Thread(target=_producer, args=(i,)) for i in range(count)]\n        for t in threads:\n            t.start()\n        for t in threads:\n            t.join()\n\n        drained = queue.drain()\n        assert len(drained) == count\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @settings(max_examples=100, deadline=None)\n    @given(count=st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=30))\n    def test_drain_then_empty(self, count):\n        \"\"\"Property: after drain(), queue is empty (second drain returns []).\"\"\"\n        queue = GateResultQueue()\n\n        for i in range(count):\n            queue.put(TrailingGateResult(\n                step_id=f\"step-{i}\", passed=True, evaluation_ms=1.0,\n            ))\n\n ===========================================================================\n# Property 3: Remediation Idempotency\n# ===========================================================================\n\n\nclass TestPropertyRemediationIdempotency:\n    \"\"\"DeferredRemediationLog.mark_remediated is idempotent.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @given(step_ids=st.lists(\n        st.text(min_size=1, max_size=20, alphabet=st.characters(\n            whitelist_categories=(\"L\", \"N\"),\n        )),\n        min_size=1,\n        max_size=30,\n        unique=True,\n    ))\n    @settings(max_examples=200, deadline=None)\n    def test_double_mark_remediated_idempotent(self, step_ids):\n        \"\"\"Property: marking the same entry remediated twice produces\n        identical state (second call is a no-op returning False).\"\"\"\n        log = DeferredRemediationLog()\n\n        # Populate with failed gate results\n        for sid in step_ids:\n            log.append(TrailingGateResult(\n                step_id=sid, passed=False,\n                evaluation_ms=1.0, failure_reason=\"test failure\",\n            ))\n\n        # Pick a random entry to remediate\n        target = step_ids[0]\n\n        # First mark: should succeed\n        result_1 = log.mark_remediated(target)\n        assert result_1 is True\n\n        # Capture state after first mark\n        pending_after_first = [e.step_id for e in log.pending_remediations()]\n\n        # Second mark: same step_id — should return False (already remediated)\n        result_2 = log.mark_remediated(target)\n        assert result_2 is False\n\n        # State unchanged: pending list is identical\n        pending_after_second = [e.step_id for e in log.pending_remediations()]\n        assert pending_after_first == pending_after_second\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @given(step_ids=st.lists(\n        st.text(min_size=1, max_size=20, alphabet=st.characters(\n            whitelist_categories=(\"L\", \"N\"),\n        )),\n        min_size=2,\n        max_size=20,\n        unique=True,\n    ))\n    @settings(max_examples=200, deadline=None)\n    def test_remediate_preserves_other_entries(self, step_ids):\n        \"\"\"Property: remediating one entry does not affect other entries.\"\"\"\n        log = DeferredRemediationLog()\n\n        for sid in step_ids:\n            log.append(TrailingGateResult(\n                step_id=sid, passed=False,\n                evaluation_ms=1.0,       unique=True,\n    ))\n    @settings(max_examples=200, deadline=None)\n    def test_serialize_deserialize_roundtrip(self, step_ids):\n        \"\"\"Property: serialize → deserialize produces identical state.\"\"\"\n        log = DeferredRemediationLog()\n\n        for sid in step_ids:\n            log.append(TrailingGateResult(\n                step_id=sid, passed=False,\n log.entry_count\n\n        # Same pending set\n        original_pending = {e.step_id for e in log.pending_remediations()}\n        restored_pending = {e.step_id for e in restored.pending_remediations()}\n        assert original_pending == restored_pending\n\n    @pytest.mark.property_based\n    @settings(max_examples=100, deadline=None)\n    @given(nonexistent_id=st.text(min_size=1, max_size=20, alphabet=st.characters(\n        whitelist_categories=(\"L\", \"N\"),\n    )))\n    def test_mark_nonexistent_returns_false(self,
